     226             : 
     227             : 
     228             : /*
     229             :    Flags/methods for returning states.
     230             : 
     231             :    The SWIG conversion methods, as ConvertPtr, return an integer
     232             :    that tells if the conversion was successful or not. And if not,
     233             :    an error code can be returned (see swigerrors.swg for the codes).
     234             : 
     235             :    Use the following macros/flags to set or process the returning
     236             :    states.
     237             : 
     238             :    In old versions of SWIG, code such as the following was usually written:
     239             : 
     240             :      if (SWIG_ConvertPtr(obj,vptr,ty.flags) != -1) {
     241             :        // success code
     242             :      } else {
     243             :        //fail code
     244             :      }
     245             : 
     246             :    Now you can be more explicit:
     247             : 
     248             :     int res = SWIG_ConvertPtr(obj,vptr,ty.flags);
     249             :     if (SWIG_IsOK(res)) {
     250             :       // success code
     251             :     } else {
     252             :       // fail code
     253             :     }
     254             : 
     255             :    which is the same really, but now you can also do
     256             : 
     257             :     Type *ptr;
     258             :     int res = SWIG_ConvertPtr(obj,(void **)(&ptr),ty.flags);
     259             :     if (SWIG_IsOK(res)) {
     260             :       // success code
     261             :       if (SWIG_IsNewObj(res) {
     262             :         ...
     263             :   delete *ptr;
     264             :       } else {
     265             :         ...
     266             :       }
     267             :     } else {
     268             :       // fail code
     269             :     }
     270             : 
     271             :    I.e., now SWIG_ConvertPtr can return new objects and you can
     272             :    identify the case and take care of the deallocation. Of course that
     273             :    also requires SWIG_ConvertPtr to return new result values, such as
     274             : 
     275             :       int SWIG_ConvertPtr(obj, ptr,...) {
     276             :         if (<obj is ok>) {
     277             :           if (<need new object>) {
     278             :             *ptr = <ptr to new allocated object>;
     279             :             return SWIG_NEWOBJ;
     280             :           } else {
     281             :             *ptr = <ptr to old object>;
     282             :             return SWIG_OLDOBJ;
     283             :           }
     284             :         } else {
     285             :           return SWIG_BADOBJ;
     286             :         }
     287             :       }
     288             : 
     289             :    Of course, returning the plain '0(success)/-1(fail)' still works, but you can be
     290             :    more explicit by returning SWIG_BADOBJ, SWIG_ERROR or any of the
     291             :    SWIG errors code.
     292             : 
     293             :    Finally, if the SWIG_CASTRANK_MODE is enabled, the result code
     294             :    allows to return the 'cast rank', for example, if you have this
     295             : 
     296             :        int food(double)
     297             :        int fooi(int);
     298             : 
     299             :    and you call
     300             : 
     301             :       food(1)   // cast rank '1'  (1 -> 1.0)
     302             :       fooi(1)   // cast rank '0'
     303             : 
     304             :    just use the SWIG_AddCast()/SWIG_CheckState()
     305             : */
     306             : 
     307             : #define SWIG_OK                    (0)
     308             : #define SWIG_ERROR                 (-1)
     309             : #define SWIG_IsOK(r)               (r >= 0)
     310             : #define SWIG_ArgError(r)           ((r != SWIG_ERROR) ? r : SWIG_TypeError)
     311             : 
     312             : /* The CastRankLimit says how many bits are used for the cast rank */
     313             : #define SWIG_CASTRANKLIMIT         (1 << 8)
     314             : /* The NewMask denotes the object was created (using new/malloc) */
     315             : #define SWIG_NEWOBJMASK            (SWIG_CASTRANKLIMIT  << 1)
     316             : /* The TmpMask is for in/out typemaps that use temporal objects */
     317             : #define SWIG_TMPOBJMASK            (SWIG_NEWOBJMASK << 1)
     318             : /* Simple returning values */
     319             : #define SWIG_BADOBJ                (SWIG_ERROR)
     320             : #define SWIG_OLDOBJ                (SWIG_OK)
     321             : #define SWIG_NEWOBJ                (SWIG_OK | SWIG_NEWOBJMASK)
     322             : #define SWIG_TMPOBJ                (SWIG_OK | SWIG_TMPOBJMASK)
     323             : /* Check, add and del mask methods */
     324             : #define SWIG_AddNewMask(r)         (SWIG_IsOK(r) ? (r | SWIG_NEWOBJMASK) : r)
     325             : #define SWIG_DelNewMask(r)         (SWIG_IsOK(r) ? (r & ~SWIG_NEWOBJMASK) : r)
     326             : #define SWIG_IsNewObj(r)           (SWIG_IsOK(r) && (r & SWIG_NEWOBJMASK))
     327             : #define SWIG_AddTmpMask(r)         (SWIG_IsOK(r) ? (r | SWIG_TMPOBJMASK) : r)
     328             : #define SWIG_DelTmpMask(r)         (SWIG_IsOK(r) ? (r & ~SWIG_TMPOBJMASK) : r)
     329             : #define SWIG_IsTmpObj(r)           (SWIG_IsOK(r) && (r & SWIG_TMPOBJMASK))
     330             : 
     331             : /* Cast-Rank Mode */
     332             : #if defined(SWIG_CASTRANK_MODE)
     333             : #  ifndef SWIG_TypeRank
     334             : #    define SWIG_TypeRank             unsigned long
     335             : #  endif
     336             : #  ifndef SWIG_MAXCASTRANK            /* Default cast allowed */
     337             : #    define SWIG_MAXCASTRANK          (2)
     338             : #  endif
     339             : #  define SWIG_CASTRANKMASK          ((SWIG_CASTRANKLIMIT) -1)
     340             : #  define SWIG_CastRank(r)           (r & SWIG_CASTRANKMASK)
     341             : SWIGINTERNINLINE int SWIG_AddCast(int r) {
     342             :   return SWIG_IsOK(r) ? ((SWIG_CastRank(r) < SWIG_MAXCASTRANK) ? (r + 1) : SWIG_ERROR) : r;
     343             : }
     344             : SWIGINTERNINLINE int SWIG_CheckState(int r) {
     345             :   return SWIG_IsOK(r) ? SWIG_CastRank(r) + 1 : 0;
     346             : }
     347             : #else /* no cast-rank mode */
     348             : #  define SWIG_AddCast(r) (r)
     349             : #  define SWIG_CheckState(r) (SWIG_IsOK(r) ? 1 : 0)
     350             : #endif

     392             : /*
     393             :   Compare two type names skipping the space characters, therefore
     394             :   "char*" == "char *" and "Class<int>" == "Class<int >", etc.
     395             : 
     396             :   Return 0 when the two name types are equivalent, as in
     397             :   strncmp, but skipping ' '.
     398             : */

     400           0 : SWIG_TypeNameComp(const char *f1, const char *l1,
     401             :       const char *f2, const char *l2) {

     403           0 :     while ((*f1 == ' ') && (f1 != l1)) ++f1;
     404           0 :     while ((*f2 == ' ') && (f2 != l2)) ++f2;
     405           0 :     if (*f1 != *f2) return (*f1 > *f2) ? 1 : -1;
     406             :   }
     407           0 :   return (int)((l1 - f1) - (l2 - f2));

     586       41367 :       do {
     587             :   /* since l+r >= 0, we can (>> 1) instead (/ 2) */

     598             :         break;

     603             :   } else {

     606             :       } while (l <= r);

     613             : /*
     614             :   Search for a swig_type_info structure for either a mangled name or a human readable name.
     615             :   It first searches the mangled names of the types, which is a O(log #types)
     616             :   If a type is not found it then searches the human readable names, which is O(#types).
     617             : 
     618             :   We start searching at module start, and finish searching when start == end.
     619             :   Note: if start == end at the beginning of the function, we go all the way around
     620             :   the circular list.

     632             :        of the str field (the human readable name) */
     633             :     swig_module_info *iter = start;
     634           0 :     do {
     635           0 :       size_t i = 0;
     636           0 :       for (; i < iter->size; ++i) {
     637           0 :   if (iter->types[i]->str && (SWIG_TypeEquiv(iter->types[i]->str, name)))

     639             :       }
     640           0 :       iter = iter->next;
     641             :     } while (iter != end);
     642             :   }
     643             : 
     644             :   /* neither found a match */
     645             :   return 0;
     646             : }

     688             :   }

    1063             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_USE_GIL
    1064             : #    endif
    1065             : #  endif
    1066             : #  if defined(SWIG_PYTHON_USE_GIL) /* Use PyGILState threads calls */
    1067             : #    ifndef SWIG_PYTHON_INITIALIZE_THREADS
    1068             : #     define SWIG_PYTHON_INITIALIZE_THREADS  PyEval_InitThreads() 
    1069             : #    endif
    1070             : #    ifdef __cplusplus /* C++ code */
    1071             :        class SWIG_Python_Thread_Block {
    1072             :          bool status;
    1073             :          PyGILState_STATE state;
    1074             :        public:
    1075           3 :          void end() { if (status) { PyGILState_Release(state); status = false;} }
    1076           3 :          SWIG_Python_Thread_Block() : status(true), state(PyGILState_Ensure()) {}
    1077             :          ~SWIG_Python_Thread_Block() { end(); }
    1078             :        };
    1079             :        class SWIG_Python_Thread_Allow {
    1080             :          bool status;
    1081             :          PyThreadState *save;
    1082             :        public:
    1083     1172642 :          void end() { if (status) { PyEval_RestoreThread(save); status = false; }}
    1084     1172642 :          SWIG_Python_Thread_Allow() : status(true), save(PyEval_SaveThread()) {}
    1085             :          ~SWIG_Python_Thread_Allow() { end(); }
    1086             :        };

    1088             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_BLOCK     _swig_thread_block.end()
    1089             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_ALLOW   SWIG_Python_Thread_Allow _swig_thread_allow
    1090             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_ALLOW     _swig_thread_allow.end()
    1091             : #    else /* C code */
    1092             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_BLOCK   PyGILState_STATE _swig_thread_block = PyGILState_Ensure()
    1093             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_BLOCK     PyGILState_Release(_swig_thread_block)
    1094             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_ALLOW   PyThreadState *_swig_thread_allow = PyEval_SaveThread()
    1095             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_ALLOW     PyEval_RestoreThread(_swig_thread_allow)
    1096             : #    endif
    1097             : #  else /* Old thread way, not implemented, user must provide it */

    1099             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_INITIALIZE_THREADS
    1100             : #    endif

    1102             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_BLOCK
    1103             : #    endif
    1104             : #    if !defined(SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_BLOCK)
    1105             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_BLOCK
    1106             : #    endif
    1107             : #    if !defined(SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_ALLOW)
    1108             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_ALLOW
    1109             : #    endif
    1110             : #    if !defined(SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_ALLOW)
    1111             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_ALLOW
    1112             : #    endif
    1113             : #  endif
    1114             : #else /* No thread support */

    1116             : #  define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_BLOCK
    1117             : #  define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_BLOCK
    1118             : #  define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_ALLOW
    1119             : #  define SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_ALLOW
    1120             : #endif

    1122             : /* -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1123             :  * Python API portion that goes into the runtime
    1124             :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    1125             : 
    1126             : #ifdef __cplusplus
    1127             : extern "C" {
    1128             : #endif
    1129             : 
    1130             : /* -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

    1133             : 
    1134             : /* Constant Types */

    1136             : #define SWIG_PY_BINARY  5
    1137             : 
    1138             : /* Constant information structure */

    1140             :   int type;

    1142             :   long lvalue;
    1143             :   double dvalue;

    1148             : 
    1149             : /* -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1150             :  * Wrapper of PyInstanceMethod_New() used in Python 3
    1151             :  * It is exported to the generated module, used for -fastproxy
    1152             :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- */

    1154             : SWIGRUNTIME PyObject* SWIG_PyInstanceMethod_New(PyObject *SWIGUNUSEDPARM(self), PyObject *func)

    1156             :   return PyInstanceMethod_New(func);

    1162             : }
    1163             : #endif
    1164             : 
    1165             : #ifdef __cplusplus
    1166             : }

    1169             : 
    1170             : /* -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1171             :  * pyrun.swg
    1172             :  *
    1173             :  * This file contains the runtime support for Python modules
    1174             :  * and includes code for managing global variables and pointer
    1175             :  * type checking.
    1176             :  *
    1177             :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    1178             : 
    1179             : /* Common SWIG API */
    1180             : 
    1181             : /* for raw pointers */
    1182             : #define SWIG_Python_ConvertPtr(obj, pptr, type, flags)  SWIG_Python_ConvertPtrAndOwn(obj, pptr, type, flags, 0)
    1183             : #define SWIG_ConvertPtr(obj, pptr, type, flags)         SWIG_Python_ConvertPtr(obj, pptr, type, flags)
    1184             : #define SWIG_ConvertPtrAndOwn(obj,pptr,type,flags,own)  SWIG_Python_ConvertPtrAndOwn(obj, pptr, type, flags, own)
    1185             : 
    1186             : #ifdef SWIGPYTHON_BUILTIN
    1187             : #define SWIG_NewPointerObj(ptr, type, flags)            SWIG_Python_NewPointerObj(self, ptr, type, flags)
    1188             : #else
    1189             : #define SWIG_NewPointerObj(ptr, type, flags)            SWIG_Python_NewPointerObj(NULL, ptr, type, flags)
    1190             : #endif
    1191             : 
    1192             : #define SWIG_InternalNewPointerObj(ptr, type, flags)  SWIG_Python_NewPointerObj(NULL, ptr, type, flags)
    1193             : 
    1194             : #define SWIG_CheckImplicit(ty)                          SWIG_Python_CheckImplicit(ty) 
    1195             : #define SWIG_AcquirePtr(ptr, src)                       SWIG_Python_AcquirePtr(ptr, src)
    1196             : #define swig_owntype                                    int
    1197             : 
    1198             : /* for raw packed data */
    1199             : #define SWIG_ConvertPacked(obj, ptr, sz, ty)            SWIG_Python_ConvertPacked(obj, ptr, sz, ty)
    1200             : #define SWIG_NewPackedObj(ptr, sz, type)                SWIG_Python_NewPackedObj(ptr, sz, type)
    1201             : 
    1202             : /* for class or struct pointers */
    1203             : #define SWIG_ConvertInstance(obj, pptr, type, flags)    SWIG_ConvertPtr(obj, pptr, type, flags)
    1204             : #define SWIG_NewInstanceObj(ptr, type, flags)           SWIG_NewPointerObj(ptr, type, flags)
    1205             : 
    1206             : /* for C or C++ function pointers */
    1207             : #define SWIG_ConvertFunctionPtr(obj, pptr, type)        SWIG_Python_ConvertFunctionPtr(obj, pptr, type)
    1208             : #define SWIG_NewFunctionPtrObj(ptr, type)               SWIG_Python_NewPointerObj(NULL, ptr, type, 0)
    1209             : 
    1210             : /* for C++ member pointers, ie, member methods */
    1211             : #define SWIG_ConvertMember(obj, ptr, sz, ty)            SWIG_Python_ConvertPacked(obj, ptr, sz, ty)
    1212             : #define SWIG_NewMemberObj(ptr, sz, type)                SWIG_Python_NewPackedObj(ptr, sz, type)
    1213             : 
    1214             : 
    1215             : /* Runtime API */
    1216             : 
    1217             : #define SWIG_GetModule(clientdata)                      SWIG_Python_GetModule(clientdata)
    1218             : #define SWIG_SetModule(clientdata, pointer)             SWIG_Python_SetModule(pointer)
    1219             : #define SWIG_NewClientData(obj)                         SwigPyClientData_New(obj)
    1220             : 
    1221             : #define SWIG_SetErrorObj                                SWIG_Python_SetErrorObj                            
    1222             : #define SWIG_SetErrorMsg                          SWIG_Python_SetErrorMsg          
    1223             : #define SWIG_ErrorType(code)                      SWIG_Python_ErrorType(code)                        
    1224             : #define SWIG_Error(code, msg)               SWIG_Python_SetErrorMsg(SWIG_ErrorType(code), msg) 
    1225             : #define SWIG_fail                           goto fail            
    1226             : 
    1227             : 
    1228             : /* Runtime API implementation */
    1229             : 
    1230             : /* Error manipulation */
    1231             : 
    1232             : SWIGINTERN void 
    1233             : SWIG_Python_SetErrorObj(PyObject *errtype, PyObject *obj) {
    1234             :   SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_BLOCK; 
    1235             :   PyErr_SetObject(errtype, obj);

    1237             :   SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_BLOCK;
    1238             : }
    1239             : 
    1240             : SWIGINTERN void 
    1241           3 : SWIG_Python_SetErrorMsg(PyObject *errtype, const char *msg) {
    1242             :   SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_BEGIN_BLOCK;

    1244             :   SWIG_PYTHON_THREAD_END_BLOCK;
    1245           3 : }
    1246             : 
    1247             : #define SWIG_Python_Raise(obj, type, desc)  SWIG_Python_SetErrorObj(SWIG_Python_ExceptionType(desc), obj)
    1248             : 
    1249             : /* Set a constant value */
    1250             : 
    1251             : #if defined(SWIGPYTHON_BUILTIN)
    1252             : 
    1253             : SWIGINTERN void
    1254             : SwigPyBuiltin_AddPublicSymbol(PyObject *seq, const char *key) {
    1255             :   PyObject *s = PyString_InternFromString(key);

    1257             :   Py_DECREF(s);
    1258             : }
    1259             : 
    1260             : SWIGINTERN void
    1261             : SWIG_Python_SetConstant(PyObject *d, PyObject *public_interface, const char *name, PyObject *obj) {   
    1262             : #if PY_VERSION_HEX < 0x02030000

    1264             : #else
    1265             :   PyDict_SetItemString(d, name, obj);
    1266             : #endif
    1267             :   Py_DECREF(obj);
    1268             :   if (public_interface)
    1269             :     SwigPyBuiltin_AddPublicSymbol(public_interface, name);
    1270             : }
    1271             : 
    1272             : #else
    1273             : 
    1274             : SWIGINTERN void
    1275       26671 : SWIG_Python_SetConstant(PyObject *d, const char *name, PyObject *obj) {

    1278             : #else
    1279       26671 :   PyDict_SetItemString(d, name, obj);
    1280             : #endif

    1284             : #endif
    1285             : 
    1286             : /* Append a value to the result obj */
    1287             : 
    1288             : SWIGINTERN PyObject*
    1289       15552 : SWIG_Python_AppendOutput(PyObject* result, PyObject* obj) {
    1290             : #if !defined(SWIG_PYTHON_OUTPUT_TUPLE)
    1291       15552 :   if (!result) {
    1292             :     result = obj;
    1293       12907 :   } else if (result == Py_None) {
    1294       12829 :     Py_DECREF(result);
    1295       12829 :     result = obj;
    1296             :   } else {
    1297          78 :     if (!PyList_Check(result)) {
    1298          13 :       PyObject *o2 = result;
    1299          13 :       result = PyList_New(1);

    1301             :     }
    1302          78 :     PyList_Append(result,obj);
    1303          78 :     Py_DECREF(obj);

    1305       15552 :   return result;
    1306             : #else
    1307             :   PyObject*   o2;
    1308             :   PyObject*   o3;
    1309             :   if (!result) {
    1310             :     result = obj;
    1311             :   } else if (result == Py_None) {
    1312             :     Py_DECREF(result);
    1313             :     result = obj;
    1314             :   } else {
    1315             :     if (!PyTuple_Check(result)) {
    1316             :       o2 = result;
    1317             :       result = PyTuple_New(1);
    1318             :       PyTuple_SET_ITEM(result, 0, o2);
    1319             :     }
    1320             :     o3 = PyTuple_New(1);
    1321             :     PyTuple_SET_ITEM(o3, 0, obj);
    1322             :     o2 = result;
    1323             :     result = PySequence_Concat(o2, o3);
    1324             :     Py_DECREF(o2);
    1325             :     Py_DECREF(o3);
    1326             :   }

    1328             : #endif

    1330             : 
    1331             : /* Unpack the argument tuple */
    1332             : 
    1333             : SWIGINTERN Py_ssize_t
    1334             : SWIG_Python_UnpackTuple(PyObject *args, const char *name, Py_ssize_t min, Py_ssize_t max, PyObject **objs)
    1335             : {
    1336             :   if (!args) {
    1337             :     if (!min && !max) {
    1338             :       return 1;
    1339             :     } else {
    1340             :       PyErr_Format(PyExc_TypeError, "%s expected %s%d arguments, got none", 
    1341             :        name, (min == max ? "" : "at least "), (int)min);
    1342             :       return 0;
    1343             :     }
    1344             :   }  
    1345             :   if (!PyTuple_Check(args)) {
    1346             :     if (min <= 1 && max >= 1) {
    1347             :       Py_ssize_t i;
    1348             :       objs[0] = args;
    1349             :       for (i = 1; i < max; ++i) {
    1350             :   objs[i] = 0;
    1351             :       }
    1352             :       return 2;
    1353             :     }
    1354             :     PyErr_SetString(PyExc_SystemError, "UnpackTuple() argument list is not a tuple");
    1355             :     return 0;
    1356             :   } else {
    1357             :     Py_ssize_t l = PyTuple_GET_SIZE(args);
    1358             :     if (l < min) {
    1359             :       PyErr_Format(PyExc_TypeError, "%s expected %s%d arguments, got %d", 
    1360             :        name, (min == max ? "" : "at least "), (int)min, (int)l);
    1361             :       return 0;
    1362             :     } else if (l > max) {
    1363             :       PyErr_Format(PyExc_TypeError, "%s expected %s%d arguments, got %d", 
    1364             :        name, (min == max ? "" : "at most "), (int)max, (int)l);
    1365             :       return 0;
    1366             :     } else {
    1367             :       Py_ssize_t i;
    1368             :       for (i = 0; i < l; ++i) {
    1369             :   objs[i] = PyTuple_GET_ITEM(args, i);
    1370             :       }
    1371             :       for (; l < max; ++l) {
    1372             :   objs[l] = 0;
    1373             :       }
    1374             :       return i + 1;
    1375             :     }    
    1376             :   }
    1377             : }
    1378             : 
    1379             : /* A functor is a function object with one single object argument */
    1380             : #if PY_VERSION_HEX >= 0x02020000
    1381             : #define SWIG_Python_CallFunctor(functor, obj)         PyObject_CallFunctionObjArgs(functor, obj, NULL);
    1382             : #else
    1383             : #define SWIG_Python_CallFunctor(functor, obj)         PyObject_CallFunction(functor, "O", obj);
    1384             : #endif

    1386             : /*
    1387             :   Helper for static pointer initialization for both C and C++ code, for example
    1388             :   static PyObject *SWIG_STATIC_POINTER(MyVar) = NewSomething(...);
    1389             : */
    1390             : #ifdef __cplusplus
    1391             : #define SWIG_STATIC_POINTER(var)  var
    1392             : #else
    1393             : #define SWIG_STATIC_POINTER(var)  var = 0; if (!var) var
    1394             : #endif

    1397             :  * Pointer declarations

    1403             : 
    1404             : #define SWIG_POINTER_IMPLICIT_CONV  (SWIG_POINTER_DISOWN   << 1)
    1405             : 
    1406             : #define SWIG_BUILTIN_TP_INIT      (SWIG_POINTER_OWN << 2)
    1407             : #define SWIG_BUILTIN_INIT     (SWIG_BUILTIN_TP_INIT | SWIG_POINTER_OWN)
    1408             : 
    1409             : #ifdef __cplusplus
    1410             : extern "C" {
    1411             : #endif
    1412             : 
    1413             : /*  How to access Py_None */

    1417             : #      define SWIG_PYTHON_BUILD_NONE
    1418             : #    endif
    1419             : #  endif
    1420             : #endif
    1421             : 
    1422             : #ifdef SWIG_PYTHON_BUILD_NONE

    1428             : _SWIG_Py_None(void)

    1432             :   return none;
    1433             : }

    1438             :   return none;
    1439             : }
    1440             : #endif

    1450             : }

    1452             : /* SwigPyClientData */
    1453             : 
    1454             : typedef struct {

    1466             : {

    1471             : SWIGRUNTIMEINLINE PyObject *
    1472             : SWIG_Python_ExceptionType(swig_type_info *desc) {

    1485        1790 :     SwigPyClientData *data = (SwigPyClientData *)malloc(sizeof(SwigPyClientData));

    1487        1790 :     data->klass = obj;

    1499             : #endif

    1540             : /* =============== SwigPyObject =====================*/
    1541             : 
    1542             : typedef struct {
    1543             :   PyObject_HEAD

    2499             : /* -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

    3856             :        diff = rd - x;
    3857             :      } else {
    3858             :        return 1;
    3859             :      }

    3865             :      }

    3867             :   }

    4563             : /* Getting isfinite working pre C99 across multiple platforms is non-trivial. Users can provide SWIG_isfinite on older platforms. */
    4564             : #ifndef SWIG_isfinite
    4565             : /* isfinite() is a macro for C99 */
    4566             : # if defined(isfinite)
    4567             : #  define SWIG_isfinite(X) (isfinite(X))
    4568             : # elif defined __cplusplus && __cplusplus >= 201103L
    4569             : /* Use a template so that this works whether isfinite() is std::isfinite() or
    4570             :  * in the global namespace.  The reality seems to vary between compiler
    4571             :  * versions.
    4572             :  *
    4573             :  * Make sure namespace std exists to avoid compiler warnings.
    4574             :  *
    4575             :  * extern "C++" is required as this fragment can end up inside an extern "C" { } block
    4576             :  */
    4577             : namespace std { }
    4578             : extern "C++" template<typename T>
    4579             : inline int SWIG_isfinite_func(T x) {
    4580             :   using namespace std;
    4581             :   return isfinite(x);
    4582             : }
    4583             : #  define SWIG_isfinite(X) (SWIG_isfinite_func(X))
    4584             : # elif defined(_MSC_VER)
    4585             : #  define SWIG_isfinite(X) (_finite(X))
    4586             : # elif defined(__sun) && defined(__SVR4)
    4587             : #  include <ieeefp.h>
    4588             : #  define SWIG_isfinite(X) (finite(X))
    4589             : # endif
    4590             : #endif
    4591             : 
    4592             : 
    4593             : /* Accept infinite as a valid float value unless we are unable to check if a value is finite */
    4594             : #ifdef SWIG_isfinite
    4595             : # define SWIG_Float_Overflow_Check(X) ((X < -FLT_MAX || X > FLT_MAX) && SWIG_isfinite(X))
    4596             : #else
    4597             : # define SWIG_Float_Overflow_Check(X) ((X < -FLT_MAX || X > FLT_MAX))
    4598             : #endif

    4715         102 :     static int ValidateOGRGeometryType(OGRwkbGeometryType field_type)
    4716             :     {
    4717         102 :         switch(field_type)
    4718             :         {
    4719             :             case wkbUnknown:
    4720             :             case wkbPoint:
    4721             :             case wkbLineString:
    4722             :             case wkbPolygon:

    4724             :             case wkbMultiLineString:
    4725             :             case wkbMultiPolygon:

   28269             :     "DataSource_GetLayerCount(DataSource self) -> int\n"
   28270             :     "\n"
   28271             :     "int\n"
   28272             :     "OGR_DS_GetLayerCount(OGRDataSourceH hDS)\n"
   28273             :     "\n"
   28274             :     "Get the number of layers in this data source.\n"
   28275             :     "\n"
   28276             :     "Deprecated Use GDALDatasetGetLayerCount() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28277             :     "\n"
   28278             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28279             :     "-----------\n"
   28280             :     "\n"
   28281             :     "hDS:  handle to the data source from which to get the number of\n"
   28282             :     "layers.\n"
   28283             :     "\n"
   28284             :     "layer count. \n"
   28285             :     ""},
   28286             :    { (char *)"DataSource_GetDriver", _wrap_DataSource_GetDriver, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28287             :     "DataSource_GetDriver(DataSource self) -> Driver\n"
   28288             :     "\n"
   28289             :     "OGRSFDriverH\n"
   28290             :     "OGR_DS_GetDriver(OGRDataSourceH hDS)\n"
   28291             :     "\n"
   28292             :     "Returns the driver that the dataset was opened with.\n"
   28293             :     "\n"
   28294             :     "NOTE: Starting with GDAL 2.0, it is NOT safe to cast the returned\n"
   28295             :     "handle to OGRSFDriver*. If a C++ object is needed, the handle should\n"
   28296             :     "be cast to GDALDriver*.\n"
   28297             :     "\n"
   28298             :     "Deprecated Use GDALGetDatasetDriver() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28299             :     "\n"
   28300             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28301             :     "-----------\n"
   28302             :     "\n"
   28303             :     "hDS:  handle to the datasource\n"
   28304             :     "\n"
   28305             :     "NULL if driver info is not available, or pointer to a driver owned by\n"
   28306             :     "the OGRSFDriverManager. \n"
   28307             :     ""},
   28308             :    { (char *)"DataSource_GetName", _wrap_DataSource_GetName, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28309             :     "DataSource_GetName(DataSource self) -> char const *\n"
   28310             :     "\n"
   28311             :     "const char*\n"
   28312             :     "OGR_DS_GetName(OGRDataSourceH hDS)\n"
   28313             :     "\n"
   28314             :     "Returns the name of the data source.\n"
   28315             :     "\n"
   28316             :     "This string should be sufficient to open the data source if passed to\n"
   28317             :     "the same OGRSFDriver that this data source was opened with, but it\n"
   28318             :     "need not be exactly the same string that was used to open the data\n"
   28319             :     "source. Normally this is a filename.\n"
   28320             :     "\n"
   28321             :     "Deprecated Use GDALGetDescription() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28322             :     "\n"
   28323             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28324             :     "-----------\n"
   28325             :     "\n"
   28326             :     "hDS:  handle to the data source to get the name from.\n"
   28327             :     "\n"
   28328             :     "pointer to an internal name string which should not be modified or\n"
   28329             :     "freed by the caller. \n"
   28330             :     ""},
   28331             :    { (char *)"DataSource_DeleteLayer", _wrap_DataSource_DeleteLayer, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28332             :     "DataSource_DeleteLayer(DataSource self, int index) -> OGRErr\n"
   28333             :     "\n"
   28334             :     "OGRErr\n"
   28335             :     "OGR_DS_DeleteLayer(OGRDataSourceH hDS, int iLayer)\n"
   28336             :     "\n"
   28337             :     "Delete the indicated layer from the datasource.\n"
   28338             :     "\n"
   28339             :     "If this method is supported the ODsCDeleteLayer capability will test\n"
   28340             :     "TRUE on the OGRDataSource.\n"
   28341             :     "\n"
   28342             :     "Deprecated Use GDALDatasetDeleteLayer() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28343             :     "\n"
   28344             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28345             :     "-----------\n"
   28346             :     "\n"
   28347             :     "hDS:  handle to the datasource\n"
   28348             :     "\n"
   28349             :     "iLayer:  the index of the layer to delete.\n"
   28350             :     "\n"
   28351             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success, or OGRERR_UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION if deleting\n"
   28352             :     "layers is not supported for this datasource. \n"
   28353             :     ""},
   28354             :    { (char *)"DataSource_SyncToDisk", _wrap_DataSource_SyncToDisk, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28355             :     "DataSource_SyncToDisk(DataSource self) -> OGRErr\n"
   28356             :     "\n"
   28357             :     "OGRErr\n"
   28358             :     "OGR_DS_SyncToDisk(OGRDataSourceH hDS)\n"
   28359             :     "\n"
   28360             :     "Flush pending changes to disk.\n"
   28361             :     "\n"
   28362             :     "See GDALDataset::FlushCache() \n"
   28363             :     ""},
   28364             :    { (char *)"DataSource_FlushCache", _wrap_DataSource_FlushCache, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"DataSource_FlushCache(DataSource self)"},
   28365             :    { (char *)"DataSource_CreateLayer", (PyCFunction) _wrap_DataSource_CreateLayer, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   28366             :     "DataSource_CreateLayer(DataSource self, char const * name, SpatialReference srs=None, OGRwkbGeometryType geom_type, char ** options=None) -> Layer\n"
   28367             :     "\n"
   28368             :     "OGRLayerH\n"
   28369             :     "OGR_DS_CreateLayer(OGRDataSourceH hDS, const char *pszName,\n"
   28370             :     "OGRSpatialReferenceH hSpatialRef, OGRwkbGeometryType eType, char\n"
   28371             :     "**papszOptions)\n"
   28372             :     "\n"
   28373             :     "This function attempts to create a new layer on the data source with\n"
   28374             :     "the indicated name, coordinate system, geometry type.\n"
   28375             :     "\n"
   28376             :     "The papszOptions argument can be used to control driver specific\n"
   28377             :     "creation options. These options are normally documented in the format\n"
   28378             :     "specific documentation.\n"
   28379             :     "\n"
   28380             :     "Deprecated Use GDALDatasetCreateLayer() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28381             :     "\n"
   28382             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28383             :     "-----------\n"
   28384             :     "\n"
   28385             :     "hDS:  The dataset handle.\n"
   28386             :     "\n"
   28387             :     "pszName:  the name for the new layer. This should ideally not match\n"
   28388             :     "any existing layer on the datasource.\n"
   28389             :     "\n"
   28390             :     "hSpatialRef:  handle to the coordinate system to use for the new\n"
   28391             :     "layer, or NULL if no coordinate system is available. The driver might\n"
   28392             :     "only increase the reference counter of the object to take ownership,\n"
   28393             :     "and not make a full copy, so do not use OSRDestroySpatialReference(),\n"
   28394             :     "but OSRRelease() instead when you are done with the object.\n"
   28395             :     "\n"
   28396             :     "eType:  the geometry type for the layer. Use wkbUnknown if there are\n"
   28397             :     "no constraints on the types geometry to be written.\n"
   28398             :     "\n"
   28399             :     "papszOptions:  a StringList of name=value options. Options are driver\n"
   28400             :     "specific, and driver information can be found at the following\n"
   28401             :     "url:http://www.gdal.org/ogr_formats.html\n"
   28402             :     "\n"
   28403             :     "NULL is returned on failure, or a new OGRLayer handle on success.\n"
   28404             :     "Example: \n"
   28405             :     ""},
   28406             :    { (char *)"DataSource_CopyLayer", (PyCFunction) _wrap_DataSource_CopyLayer, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   28407             :     "DataSource_CopyLayer(DataSource self, Layer src_layer, char const * new_name, char ** options=None) -> Layer\n"
   28408             :     "\n"
   28409             :     "OGRLayerH\n"
   28410             :     "OGR_DS_CopyLayer(OGRDataSourceH hDS, OGRLayerH hSrcLayer, const char\n"
   28411             :     "*pszNewName, char **papszOptions)\n"
   28412             :     "\n"
   28413             :     "Duplicate an existing layer.\n"
   28414             :     "\n"
   28415             :     "This function creates a new layer, duplicate the field definitions of\n"
   28416             :     "the source layer and then duplicate each features of the source layer.\n"
   28417             :     "The papszOptions argument can be used to control driver specific\n"
   28418             :     "creation options. These options are normally documented in the format\n"
   28419             :     "specific documentation. The source layer may come from another\n"
   28420             :     "dataset.\n"
   28421             :     "\n"
   28422             :     "Deprecated Use GDALDatasetCopyLayer() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28423             :     "\n"
   28424             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28425             :     "-----------\n"
   28426             :     "\n"
   28427             :     "hDS:  handle to the data source where to create the new layer\n"
   28428             :     "\n"
   28429             :     "hSrcLayer:  handle to the source layer.\n"
   28430             :     "\n"
   28431             :     "pszNewName:  the name of the layer to create.\n"
   28432             :     "\n"
   28433             :     "papszOptions:  a StringList of name=value options. Options are driver\n"
   28434             :     "specific.\n"
   28435             :     "\n"
   28436             :     "an handle to the layer, or NULL if an error occurs. \n"
   28437             :     ""},
   28438             :    { (char *)"DataSource_GetLayerByIndex", _wrap_DataSource_GetLayerByIndex, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"DataSource_GetLayerByIndex(DataSource self, int index=0) -> Layer"},
   28439             :    { (char *)"DataSource_GetLayerByName", _wrap_DataSource_GetLayerByName, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28440             :     "DataSource_GetLayerByName(DataSource self, char const * layer_name) -> Layer\n"
   28441             :     "\n"
   28442             :     "OGRLayerH\n"
   28443             :     "OGR_DS_GetLayerByName(OGRDataSourceH hDS, const char *pszName)\n"
   28444             :     "\n"
   28445             :     "Fetch a layer by name.\n"
   28446             :     "\n"
   28447             :     "The returned layer remains owned by the OGRDataSource and should not\n"
   28448             :     "be deleted by the application.\n"
   28449             :     "\n"
   28450             :     "Deprecated Use GDALDatasetGetLayerByName() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28451             :     "\n"
   28452             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28453             :     "-----------\n"
   28454             :     "\n"
   28455             :     "hDS:  handle to the data source from which to get the layer.\n"
   28456             :     "\n"
   28457             :     "pszLayerName:  Layer the layer name of the layer to fetch.\n"
   28458             :     "\n"
   28459             :     "an handle to the layer, or NULL if the layer is not found or an error\n"
   28460             :     "occurs. \n"
   28461             :     ""},
   28462             :    { (char *)"DataSource_TestCapability", _wrap_DataSource_TestCapability, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28463             :     "DataSource_TestCapability(DataSource self, char const * cap) -> bool\n"
   28464             :     "\n"
   28465             :     "int\n"
   28466             :     "OGR_DS_TestCapability(OGRDataSourceH hDS, const char *pszCap)\n"
   28467             :     "\n"
   28468             :     "Test if capability is available.\n"
   28469             :     "\n"
   28470             :     "One of the following data source capability names can be passed into\n"
   28471             :     "this function, and a TRUE or FALSE value will be returned indicating\n"
   28472             :     "whether or not the capability is available for this object.\n"
   28473             :     "\n"
   28474             :     "ODsCCreateLayer: True if this datasource can create new layers.\n"
   28475             :     "\n"
   28476             :     "ODsCDeleteLayer: True if this datasource can delete existing layers.\n"
   28477             :     "\n"
   28478             :     "ODsCCreateGeomFieldAfterCreateLayer: True if the layers of this\n"
   28479             :     "datasource support CreateGeomField() just after layer creation.\n"
   28480             :     "\n"
   28481             :     "ODsCCurveGeometries: True if this datasource supports writing curve\n"
   28482             :     "geometries. (GDAL 2.0). In that case, OLCCurveGeometries must also be\n"
   28483             :     "declared in layers of that dataset.\n"
   28484             :     "\n"
   28485             :     "The #define macro forms of the capability names should be used in\n"
   28486             :     "preference to the strings themselves to avoid misspelling.\n"
   28487             :     "\n"
   28488             :     "Deprecated Use GDALDatasetTestCapability() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28489             :     "\n"
   28490             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28491             :     "-----------\n"
   28492             :     "\n"
   28493             :     "hDS:  handle to the data source against which to test the capability.\n"
   28494             :     "\n"
   28495             :     "pszCapability:  the capability to test.\n"
   28496             :     "\n"
   28497             :     "TRUE if capability available otherwise FALSE. \n"
   28498             :     ""},
   28499             :    { (char *)"DataSource_ExecuteSQL", (PyCFunction) _wrap_DataSource_ExecuteSQL, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   28500             :     "DataSource_ExecuteSQL(DataSource self, char const * statement, Geometry spatialFilter=None, char const * dialect) -> Layer\n"
   28501             :     "\n"
   28502             :     "OGRLayerH\n"
   28503             :     "OGR_DS_ExecuteSQL(OGRDataSourceH hDS, const char *pszStatement,\n"
   28504             :     "OGRGeometryH hSpatialFilter, const char *pszDialect)\n"
   28505             :     "\n"
   28506             :     "Execute an SQL statement against the data store.\n"
   28507             :     "\n"
   28508             :     "The result of an SQL query is either NULL for statements that are in\n"
   28509             :     "error, or that have no results set, or an OGRLayer handle representing\n"
   28510             :     "a results set from the query. Note that this OGRLayer is in addition\n"
   28511             :     "to the layers in the data store and must be destroyed with\n"
   28512             :     "OGR_DS_ReleaseResultSet() before the data source is closed\n"
   28513             :     "(destroyed).\n"
   28514             :     "\n"
   28515             :     "For more information on the SQL dialect supported internally by OGR\n"
   28516             :     "review theOGR SQL document. Some drivers (i.e. Oracle and PostGIS)\n"
   28517             :     "pass the SQL directly through to the underlying RDBMS.\n"
   28518             :     "\n"
   28519             :     "Starting with OGR 1.10, theSQLITE dialect can also be used.\n"
   28520             :     "\n"
   28521             :     "Deprecated Use GDALDatasetExecuteSQL() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28522             :     "\n"
   28523             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28524             :     "-----------\n"
   28525             :     "\n"
   28526             :     "hDS:  handle to the data source on which the SQL query is executed.\n"
   28527             :     "\n"
   28528             :     "pszSQLCommand:  the SQL statement to execute.\n"
   28529             :     "\n"
   28530             :     "hSpatialFilter:  handle to a geometry which represents a spatial\n"
   28531             :     "filter. Can be NULL.\n"
   28532             :     "\n"
   28533             :     "pszDialect:  allows control of the statement dialect. If set to NULL,\n"
   28534             :     "the OGR SQL engine will be used, except for RDBMS drivers that will\n"
   28535             :     "use their dedicated SQL engine, unless OGRSQL is explicitly passed as\n"
   28536             :     "the dialect. Starting with OGR 1.10, the SQLITE dialect can also be\n"
   28537             :     "used.\n"
   28538             :     "\n"
   28539             :     "an handle to a OGRLayer containing the results of the query.\n"
   28540             :     "Deallocate with OGR_DS_ReleaseResultSet(). \n"
   28541             :     ""},
   28542             :    { (char *)"DataSource_ReleaseResultSet", _wrap_DataSource_ReleaseResultSet, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28543             :     "DataSource_ReleaseResultSet(DataSource self, Layer layer)\n"
   28544             :     "\n"
   28545             :     "void\n"
   28546             :     "OGR_DS_ReleaseResultSet(OGRDataSourceH hDS, OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   28547             :     "\n"
   28548             :     "Release results of OGR_DS_ExecuteSQL().\n"
   28549             :     "\n"
   28550             :     "This function should only be used to deallocate OGRLayers resulting\n"
   28551             :     "from an OGR_DS_ExecuteSQL() call on the same OGRDataSource. Failure to\n"
   28552             :     "deallocate a results set before destroying the OGRDataSource may cause\n"
   28553             :     "errors.\n"
   28554             :     "\n"
   28555             :     "Deprecated Use GDALDatasetReleaseResultSet() in GDAL 2.0\n"
   28556             :     "\n"
   28557             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28558             :     "-----------\n"
   28559             :     "\n"
   28560             :     "hDS:  an handle to the data source on which was executed an SQL query.\n"
   28561             :     "\n"
   28562             :     "hLayer:  handle to the result of a previous OGR_DS_ExecuteSQL() call.\n"
   28563             :     "\n"
   28564             :     ""},
   28565             :    { (char *)"DataSource_GetStyleTable", _wrap_DataSource_GetStyleTable, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28566             :     "DataSource_GetStyleTable(DataSource self) -> StyleTable\n"
   28567             :     "\n"
   28568             :     "OGRStyleTableH\n"
   28569             :     "OGR_DS_GetStyleTable(OGRDataSourceH hDS)\n"
   28570             :     "\n"
   28571             :     "Get style table. \n"
   28572             :     ""},
   28573             :    { (char *)"DataSource_SetStyleTable", _wrap_DataSource_SetStyleTable, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28574             :     "DataSource_SetStyleTable(DataSource self, StyleTable table)\n"
   28575             :     "\n"
   28576             :     "void\n"
   28577             :     "OGR_DS_SetStyleTable(OGRDataSourceH hDS, OGRStyleTableH hStyleTable)\n"
   28578             :     "\n"
   28579             :     "Set style table. \n"
   28580             :     ""},
   28581             :    { (char *)"DataSource_StartTransaction", (PyCFunction) _wrap_DataSource_StartTransaction, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"DataSource_StartTransaction(DataSource self, int force=False) -> OGRErr"},
   28582             :    { (char *)"DataSource_CommitTransaction", _wrap_DataSource_CommitTransaction, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"DataSource_CommitTransaction(DataSource self) -> OGRErr"},
   28583             :    { (char *)"DataSource_RollbackTransaction", _wrap_DataSource_RollbackTransaction, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"DataSource_RollbackTransaction(DataSource self) -> OGRErr"},
   28584             :    { (char *)"DataSource_swigregister", DataSource_swigregister, METH_VARARGS, NULL},
   28585             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetRefCount", _wrap_Layer_GetRefCount, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28586             :     "Layer_GetRefCount(Layer self) -> int\n"
   28587             :     "\n"
   28588             :     "int OGR_L_GetRefCount(OGRLayerH\n"
   28589             :     "hLayer) \n"
   28590             :     ""},
   28591             :    { (char *)"Layer_SetSpatialFilter", _wrap_Layer_SetSpatialFilter, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28592             :     "SetSpatialFilter(Geometry filter)\n"
   28593             :     "Layer_SetSpatialFilter(Layer self, int iGeomField, Geometry filter)\n"
   28594             :     "\n"
   28595             :     "void\n"
   28596             :     "OGR_L_SetSpatialFilter(OGRLayerH hLayer, OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   28597             :     "\n"
   28598             :     "Set a new spatial filter.\n"
   28599             :     "\n"
   28600             :     "This function set the geometry to be used as a spatial filter when\n"
   28601             :     "fetching features via the OGR_L_GetNextFeature() function. Only\n"
   28602             :     "features that geometrically intersect the filter geometry will be\n"
   28603             :     "returned.\n"
   28604             :     "\n"
   28605             :     "Currently this test is may be inaccurately implemented, but it is\n"
   28606             :     "guaranteed that all features whose envelope (as returned by\n"
   28607             :     "OGR_G_GetEnvelope()) overlaps the envelope of the spatial filter will\n"
   28608             :     "be returned. This can result in more shapes being returned that should\n"
   28609             :     "strictly be the case.\n"
   28610             :     "\n"
   28611             :     "Starting with GDAL 2.3, features with null or empty geometries will\n"
   28612             :     "never be considered as matching a spatial filter.\n"
   28613             :     "\n"
   28614             :     "This function makes an internal copy of the passed geometry. The\n"
   28615             :     "passed geometry remains the responsibility of the caller, and may be\n"
   28616             :     "safely destroyed.\n"
   28617             :     "\n"
   28618             :     "For the time being the passed filter geometry should be in the same\n"
   28619             :     "SRS as the layer (as returned by OGR_L_GetSpatialRef()). In the future\n"
   28620             :     "this may be generalized.\n"
   28621             :     "\n"
   28622             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   28623             :     "OGRLayer::SetSpatialFilter.\n"
   28624             :     "\n"
   28625             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28626             :     "-----------\n"
   28627             :     "\n"
   28628             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer on which to set the spatial filter.\n"
   28629             :     "\n"
   28630             :     "hGeom:  handle to the geometry to use as a filtering region. NULL may\n"
   28631             :     "be passed indicating that the current spatial filter should be\n"
   28632             :     "cleared, but no new one instituted. \n"
   28633             :     ""},
   28634             :    { (char *)"Layer_SetSpatialFilterRect", _wrap_Layer_SetSpatialFilterRect, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28635             :     "SetSpatialFilterRect(double minx, double miny, double maxx, double maxy)\n"
   28636             :     "Layer_SetSpatialFilterRect(Layer self, int iGeomField, double minx, double miny, double maxx, double maxy)\n"
   28637             :     "\n"
   28638             :     "void\n"
   28639             :     "OGR_L_SetSpatialFilterRect(OGRLayerH hLayer, double dfMinX, double\n"
   28640             :     "dfMinY, double dfMaxX, double dfMaxY)\n"
   28641             :     "\n"
   28642             :     "Set a new rectangular spatial filter.\n"
   28643             :     "\n"
   28644             :     "This method set rectangle to be used as a spatial filter when fetching\n"
   28645             :     "features via the OGR_L_GetNextFeature() method. Only features that\n"
   28646             :     "geometrically intersect the given rectangle will be returned.\n"
   28647             :     "\n"
   28648             :     "The x/y values should be in the same coordinate system as the layer as\n"
   28649             :     "a whole (as returned by OGRLayer::GetSpatialRef()). Internally this\n"
   28650             :     "method is normally implemented as creating a 5 vertex closed\n"
   28651             :     "rectangular polygon and passing it to OGRLayer::SetSpatialFilter(). It\n"
   28652             :     "exists as a convenience.\n"
   28653             :     "\n"
   28654             :     "The only way to clear a spatial filter set with this method is to call\n"
   28655             :     "OGRLayer::SetSpatialFilter(NULL).\n"
   28656             :     "\n"
   28657             :     "This method is the same as the C++ method\n"
   28658             :     "OGRLayer::SetSpatialFilterRect().\n"
   28659             :     "\n"
   28660             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28661             :     "-----------\n"
   28662             :     "\n"
   28663             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer on which to set the spatial filter.\n"
   28664             :     "\n"
   28665             :     "dfMinX:  the minimum X coordinate for the rectangular region.\n"
   28666             :     "\n"
   28667             :     "dfMinY:  the minimum Y coordinate for the rectangular region.\n"
   28668             :     "\n"
   28669             :     "dfMaxX:  the maximum X coordinate for the rectangular region.\n"
   28670             :     "\n"
   28671             :     "dfMaxY:  the maximum Y coordinate for the rectangular region. \n"
   28672             :     ""},
   28673             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetSpatialFilter", _wrap_Layer_GetSpatialFilter, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28674             :     "Layer_GetSpatialFilter(Layer self) -> Geometry\n"
   28675             :     "\n"
   28676             :     "OGRGeometryH\n"
   28677             :     "OGR_L_GetSpatialFilter(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   28678             :     "\n"
   28679             :     "This function returns the current spatial filter for this layer.\n"
   28680             :     "\n"
   28681             :     "The returned pointer is to an internally owned object, and should not\n"
   28682             :     "be altered or deleted by the caller.\n"
   28683             :     "\n"
   28684             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   28685             :     "OGRLayer::GetSpatialFilter().\n"
   28686             :     "\n"
   28687             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28688             :     "-----------\n"
   28689             :     "\n"
   28690             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer to get the spatial filter from.\n"
   28691             :     "\n"
   28692             :     "an handle to the spatial filter geometry. \n"
   28693             :     ""},
   28694             :    { (char *)"Layer_SetAttributeFilter", _wrap_Layer_SetAttributeFilter, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28695             :     "Layer_SetAttributeFilter(Layer self, char * filter_string) -> OGRErr\n"
   28696             :     "\n"
   28697             :     "OGRErr\n"
   28698             :     "OGR_L_SetAttributeFilter(OGRLayerH hLayer, const char *pszQuery)\n"
   28699             :     "\n"
   28700             :     "Set a new attribute query.\n"
   28701             :     "\n"
   28702             :     "This function sets the attribute query string to be used when fetching\n"
   28703             :     "features via the OGR_L_GetNextFeature() function. Only features for\n"
   28704             :     "which the query evaluates as true will be returned.\n"
   28705             :     "\n"
   28706             :     "The query string should be in the format of an SQL WHERE clause. For\n"
   28707             :     "instance \"population > 1000000 and population < 5000000\" where\n"
   28708             :     "population is an attribute in the layer. The query format is a\n"
   28709             :     "restricted form of SQL WHERE clause as defined\n"
   28710             :     "\"eq_format=restricted_where\" about half way through this document:\n"
   28711             :     "\n"
   28712             :     "http://ogdi.sourceforge.net/prop/6.2.CapabilitiesMetadata.html\n"
   28713             :     "\n"
   28714             :     "Note that installing a query string will generally result in resetting\n"
   28715             :     "the current reading position (ala OGR_L_ResetReading()).\n"
   28716             :     "\n"
   28717             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   28718             :     "OGRLayer::SetAttributeFilter().\n"
   28719             :     "\n"
   28720             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28721             :     "-----------\n"
   28722             :     "\n"
   28723             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer on which attribute query will be\n"
   28724             :     "executed.\n"
   28725             :     "\n"
   28726             :     "pszQuery:  query in restricted SQL WHERE format, or NULL to clear the\n"
   28727             :     "current query.\n"
   28728             :     "\n"
   28729             :     "OGRERR_NONE if successfully installed, or an error code if the query\n"
   28730             :     "expression is in error, or some other failure occurs. \n"
   28731             :     ""},
   28732             :    { (char *)"Layer_ResetReading", _wrap_Layer_ResetReading, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28733             :     "Layer_ResetReading(Layer self)\n"
   28734             :     "\n"
   28735             :     "void\n"
   28736             :     "OGR_L_ResetReading(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   28737             :     "\n"
   28738             :     "Reset feature reading to start on the first feature.\n"
   28739             :     "\n"
   28740             :     "This affects GetNextFeature().\n"
   28741             :     "\n"
   28742             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::ResetReading().\n"
   28743             :     "\n"
   28744             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28745             :     "-----------\n"
   28746             :     "\n"
   28747             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer on which features are read. \n"
   28748             :     ""},
   28749             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetName", _wrap_Layer_GetName, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28750             :     "Layer_GetName(Layer self) -> char const *\n"
   28751             :     "\n"
   28752             :     "const char* OGR_L_GetName(OGRLayerH\n"
   28753             :     "hLayer)\n"
   28754             :     "\n"
   28755             :     "Return the layer name.\n"
   28756             :     "\n"
   28757             :     "This returns the same content as\n"
   28758             :     "OGR_FD_GetName(OGR_L_GetLayerDefn(hLayer)), but for a few drivers,\n"
   28759             :     "calling OGR_L_GetName() directly can avoid lengthy layer definition\n"
   28760             :     "initialization.\n"
   28761             :     "\n"
   28762             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::GetName().\n"
   28763             :     "\n"
   28764             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28765             :     "-----------\n"
   28766             :     "\n"
   28767             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer.\n"
   28768             :     "\n"
   28769             :     "the layer name (must not been freed)\n"
   28770             :     "\n"
   28771             :     "OGR 1.8.0 \n"
   28772             :     ""},
   28773             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetGeomType", _wrap_Layer_GetGeomType, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28774             :     "Layer_GetGeomType(Layer self) -> OGRwkbGeometryType\n"
   28775             :     "\n"
   28776             :     "OGRwkbGeometryType\n"
   28777             :     "OGR_L_GetGeomType(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   28778             :     "\n"
   28779             :     "Return the layer geometry type.\n"
   28780             :     "\n"
   28781             :     "This returns the same result as\n"
   28782             :     "OGR_FD_GetGeomType(OGR_L_GetLayerDefn(hLayer)), but for a few drivers,\n"
   28783             :     "calling OGR_L_GetGeomType() directly can avoid lengthy layer\n"
   28784             :     "definition initialization.\n"
   28785             :     "\n"
   28786             :     "For layers with multiple geometry fields, this method only returns the\n"
   28787             :     "geometry type of the first geometry column. For other columns, use\n"
   28788             :     "OGR_GFld_GetType(OGR_FD_GetGeomFieldDefn(OGR_L_GetLayerDefn(hLayer),\n"
   28789             :     "i)). For layers without any geometry field, this method returns\n"
   28790             :     "wkbNone.\n"
   28791             :     "\n"
   28792             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::GetGeomType().\n"
   28793             :     "\n"
   28794             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28795             :     "-----------\n"
   28796             :     "\n"
   28797             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer.\n"
   28798             :     "\n"
   28799             :     "the geometry type\n"
   28800             :     "\n"
   28801             :     "OGR 1.8.0 \n"
   28802             :     ""},
   28803             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetGeometryColumn", _wrap_Layer_GetGeometryColumn, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28804             :     "Layer_GetGeometryColumn(Layer self) -> char const *\n"
   28805             :     "\n"
   28806             :     "const char*\n"
   28807             :     "OGR_L_GetGeometryColumn(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   28808             :     "\n"
   28809             :     "This method returns the name of the underlying database column being\n"
   28810             :     "used as the geometry column, or \"\" if not supported.\n"
   28811             :     "\n"
   28812             :     "For layers with multiple geometry fields, this method only returns the\n"
   28813             :     "geometry type of the first geometry column. For other columns, use OGR\n"
   28814             :     "_GFld_GetNameRef(OGR_FD_GetGeomFieldDefn(OGR_L_GetLayerDefn(hLayer),\n"
   28815             :     "i)).\n"
   28816             :     "\n"
   28817             :     "This method is the same as the C++ method\n"
   28818             :     "OGRLayer::GetGeometryColumn()\n"
   28819             :     "\n"
   28820             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28821             :     "-----------\n"
   28822             :     "\n"
   28823             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer\n"
   28824             :     "\n"
   28825             :     "geometry column name. \n"
   28826             :     ""},
   28827             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetFIDColumn", _wrap_Layer_GetFIDColumn, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28828             :     "Layer_GetFIDColumn(Layer self) -> char const *\n"
   28829             :     "\n"
   28830             :     "const char*\n"
   28831             :     "OGR_L_GetFIDColumn(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   28832             :     "\n"
   28833             :     "This method returns the name of the underlying database column being\n"
   28834             :     "used as the FID column, or \"\" if not supported.\n"
   28835             :     "\n"
   28836             :     "This method is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::GetFIDColumn()\n"
   28837             :     "\n"
   28838             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28839             :     "-----------\n"
   28840             :     "\n"
   28841             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer\n"
   28842             :     "\n"
   28843             :     "fid column name. \n"
   28844             :     ""},
   28845             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetFeature", _wrap_Layer_GetFeature, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28846             :     "Layer_GetFeature(Layer self, GIntBig fid) -> Feature\n"
   28847             :     "\n"
   28848             :     "OGRFeatureH\n"
   28849             :     "OGR_L_GetFeature(OGRLayerH hLayer, GIntBig nFeatureId)\n"
   28850             :     "\n"
   28851             :     "Fetch a feature by its identifier.\n"
   28852             :     "\n"
   28853             :     "This function will attempt to read the identified feature. The nFID\n"
   28854             :     "value cannot be OGRNullFID. Success or failure of this operation is\n"
   28855             :     "unaffected by the spatial or attribute filters (and specialized\n"
   28856             :     "implementations in drivers should make sure that they do not take into\n"
   28857             :     "account spatial or attribute filters).\n"
   28858             :     "\n"
   28859             :     "If this function returns a non-NULL feature, it is guaranteed that its\n"
   28860             :     "feature id ( OGR_F_GetFID()) will be the same as nFID.\n"
   28861             :     "\n"
   28862             :     "Use OGR_L_TestCapability(OLCRandomRead) to establish if this layer\n"
   28863             :     "supports efficient random access reading via OGR_L_GetFeature();\n"
   28864             :     "however, the call should always work if the feature exists as a\n"
   28865             :     "fallback implementation just scans all the features in the layer\n"
   28866             :     "looking for the desired feature.\n"
   28867             :     "\n"
   28868             :     "Sequential reads (with OGR_L_GetNextFeature()) are generally\n"
   28869             :     "considered interrupted by a OGR_L_GetFeature() call.\n"
   28870             :     "\n"
   28871             :     "The returned feature should be free with OGR_F_Destroy().\n"
   28872             :     "\n"
   28873             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::GetFeature( ).\n"
   28874             :     "\n"
   28875             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28876             :     "-----------\n"
   28877             :     "\n"
   28878             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer that owned the feature.\n"
   28879             :     "\n"
   28880             :     "nFeatureId:  the feature id of the feature to read.\n"
   28881             :     "\n"
   28882             :     "an handle to a feature now owned by the caller, or NULL on failure. \n"
   28883             :     ""},
   28884             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetNextFeature", _wrap_Layer_GetNextFeature, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28885             :     "Layer_GetNextFeature(Layer self) -> Feature\n"
   28886             :     "\n"
   28887             :     "OGRFeatureH\n"
   28888             :     "OGR_L_GetNextFeature(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   28889             :     "\n"
   28890             :     "Fetch the next available feature from this layer.\n"
   28891             :     "\n"
   28892             :     "The returned feature becomes the responsibility of the caller to\n"
   28893             :     "delete with OGR_F_Destroy(). It is critical that all features\n"
   28894             :     "associated with an OGRLayer (more specifically an OGRFeatureDefn) be\n"
   28895             :     "deleted before that layer/datasource is deleted.\n"
   28896             :     "\n"
   28897             :     "Only features matching the current spatial filter (set with\n"
   28898             :     "SetSpatialFilter()) will be returned.\n"
   28899             :     "\n"
   28900             :     "This function implements sequential access to the features of a layer.\n"
   28901             :     "The OGR_L_ResetReading() function can be used to start at the\n"
   28902             :     "beginning again.\n"
   28903             :     "\n"
   28904             :     "Features returned by OGR_GetNextFeature() may or may not be affected\n"
   28905             :     "by concurrent modifications depending on drivers. A guaranteed way of\n"
   28906             :     "seeing modifications in effect is to call OGR_L_ResetReading() on\n"
   28907             :     "layers where OGR_GetNextFeature() has been called, before reading\n"
   28908             :     "again. Structural changes in layers (field addition, deletion, ...)\n"
   28909             :     "when a read is in progress may or may not be possible depending on\n"
   28910             :     "drivers. If a transaction is committed/aborted, the current sequential\n"
   28911             :     "reading may or may not be valid after that operation and a call to\n"
   28912             :     "OGR_L_ResetReading() might be needed.\n"
   28913             :     "\n"
   28914             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   28915             :     "OGRLayer::GetNextFeature().\n"
   28916             :     "\n"
   28917             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28918             :     "-----------\n"
   28919             :     "\n"
   28920             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer from which feature are read.\n"
   28921             :     "\n"
   28922             :     "an handle to a feature, or NULL if no more features are available. \n"
   28923             :     ""},
   28924             :    { (char *)"Layer_SetNextByIndex", _wrap_Layer_SetNextByIndex, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28925             :     "Layer_SetNextByIndex(Layer self, GIntBig new_index) -> OGRErr\n"
   28926             :     "\n"
   28927             :     "OGRErr\n"
   28928             :     "OGR_L_SetNextByIndex(OGRLayerH hLayer, GIntBig nIndex)\n"
   28929             :     "\n"
   28930             :     "Move read cursor to the nIndex'th feature in the current resultset.\n"
   28931             :     "\n"
   28932             :     "This method allows positioning of a layer such that the\n"
   28933             :     "GetNextFeature() call will read the requested feature, where nIndex is\n"
   28934             :     "an absolute index into the current result set. So, setting it to 3\n"
   28935             :     "would mean the next feature read with GetNextFeature() would have been\n"
   28936             :     "the 4th feature to have been read if sequential reading took place\n"
   28937             :     "from the beginning of the layer, including accounting for spatial and\n"
   28938             :     "attribute filters.\n"
   28939             :     "\n"
   28940             :     "Only in rare circumstances is SetNextByIndex() efficiently\n"
   28941             :     "implemented. In all other cases the default implementation which calls\n"
   28942             :     "ResetReading() and then calls GetNextFeature() nIndex times is used.\n"
   28943             :     "To determine if fast seeking is available on the current layer use the\n"
   28944             :     "TestCapability() method with a value of OLCFastSetNextByIndex.\n"
   28945             :     "\n"
   28946             :     "This method is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::SetNextByIndex()\n"
   28947             :     "\n"
   28948             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28949             :     "-----------\n"
   28950             :     "\n"
   28951             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer\n"
   28952             :     "\n"
   28953             :     "nIndex:  the index indicating how many steps into the result set to\n"
   28954             :     "seek.\n"
   28955             :     "\n"
   28956             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success or an error code. \n"
   28957             :     ""},
   28958             :    { (char *)"Layer_SetFeature", _wrap_Layer_SetFeature, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28959             :     "Layer_SetFeature(Layer self, Feature feature) -> OGRErr\n"
   28960             :     "\n"
   28961             :     "OGRErr OGR_L_SetFeature(OGRLayerH\n"
   28962             :     "hLayer, OGRFeatureH hFeat)\n"
   28963             :     "\n"
   28964             :     "Rewrite an existing feature.\n"
   28965             :     "\n"
   28966             :     "This function will write a feature to the layer, based on the feature\n"
   28967             :     "id within the OGRFeature.\n"
   28968             :     "\n"
   28969             :     "Use OGR_L_TestCapability(OLCRandomWrite) to establish if this layer\n"
   28970             :     "supports random access writing via OGR_L_SetFeature().\n"
   28971             :     "\n"
   28972             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::SetFeature().\n"
   28973             :     "\n"
   28974             :     "Parameters:\n"
   28975             :     "-----------\n"
   28976             :     "\n"
   28977             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer to write the feature.\n"
   28978             :     "\n"
   28979             :     "hFeat:  the feature to write.\n"
   28980             :     "\n"
   28981             :     "OGRERR_NONE if the operation works, otherwise an appropriate error\n"
   28982             :     "code (e.g OGRERR_NON_EXISTING_FEATURE if the feature does not exist).\n"
   28983             :     "\n"
   28984             :     ""},
   28985             :    { (char *)"Layer_CreateFeature", _wrap_Layer_CreateFeature, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   28986             :     "Layer_CreateFeature(Layer self, Feature feature) -> OGRErr\n"
   28987             :     "\n"
   28988             :     "OGRErr\n"
   28989             :     "OGR_L_CreateFeature(OGRLayerH hLayer, OGRFeatureH hFeat)\n"
   28990             :     "\n"
   28991             :     "Create and write a new feature within a layer.\n"
   28992             :     "\n"
   28993             :     "The passed feature is written to the layer as a new feature, rather\n"
   28994             :     "than overwriting an existing one. If the feature has a feature id\n"
   28995             :     "other than OGRNullFID, then the native implementation may use that as\n"
   28996             :     "the feature id of the new feature, but not necessarily. Upon\n"
   28997             :     "successful return the passed feature will have been updated with the\n"
   28998             :     "new feature id.\n"
   28999             :     "\n"
   29000             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::CreateFeature().\n"
   29001             :     "\n"
   29002             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29003             :     "-----------\n"
   29004             :     "\n"
   29005             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer to write the feature to.\n"
   29006             :     "\n"
   29007             :     "hFeat:  the handle of the feature to write to disk.\n"
   29008             :     "\n"
   29009             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success. \n"
   29010             :     ""},
   29011             :    { (char *)"Layer_DeleteFeature", _wrap_Layer_DeleteFeature, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29012             :     "Layer_DeleteFeature(Layer self, GIntBig fid) -> OGRErr\n"
   29013             :     "\n"
   29014             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29015             :     "OGR_L_DeleteFeature(OGRLayerH hLayer, GIntBig nFID)\n"
   29016             :     "\n"
   29017             :     "Delete feature from layer.\n"
   29018             :     "\n"
   29019             :     "The feature with the indicated feature id is deleted from the layer if\n"
   29020             :     "supported by the driver. Most drivers do not support feature deletion,\n"
   29021             :     "and will return OGRERR_UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION. The\n"
   29022             :     "OGR_L_TestCapability() function may be called with OLCDeleteFeature to\n"
   29023             :     "check if the driver supports feature deletion.\n"
   29024             :     "\n"
   29025             :     "This method is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::DeleteFeature().\n"
   29026             :     "\n"
   29027             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29028             :     "-----------\n"
   29029             :     "\n"
   29030             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer\n"
   29031             :     "\n"
   29032             :     "nFID:  the feature id to be deleted from the layer\n"
   29033             :     "\n"
