   29034             :     "OGRERR_NONE if the operation works, otherwise an appropriate error\n"
   29035             :     "code (e.g OGRERR_NON_EXISTING_FEATURE if the feature does not exist).\n"
   29036             :     "\n"
   29037             :     ""},
   29038             :    { (char *)"Layer_SyncToDisk", _wrap_Layer_SyncToDisk, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29039             :     "Layer_SyncToDisk(Layer self) -> OGRErr\n"
   29040             :     "\n"
   29041             :     "OGRErr OGR_L_SyncToDisk(OGRLayerH\n"
   29042             :     "hLayer)\n"
   29043             :     "\n"
   29044             :     "Flush pending changes to disk.\n"
   29045             :     "\n"
   29046             :     "This call is intended to force the layer to flush any pending writes\n"
   29047             :     "to disk, and leave the disk file in a consistent state. It would not\n"
   29048             :     "normally have any effect on read-only datasources.\n"
   29049             :     "\n"
   29050             :     "Some layers do not implement this method, and will still return\n"
   29051             :     "OGRERR_NONE. The default implementation just returns OGRERR_NONE. An\n"
   29052             :     "error is only returned if an error occurs while attempting to flush to\n"
   29053             :     "disk.\n"
   29054             :     "\n"
   29055             :     "In any event, you should always close any opened datasource with\n"
   29056             :     "OGR_DS_Destroy() that will ensure all data is correctly flushed.\n"
   29057             :     "\n"
   29058             :     "This method is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::SyncToDisk()\n"
   29059             :     "\n"
   29060             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29061             :     "-----------\n"
   29062             :     "\n"
   29063             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer\n"
   29064             :     "\n"
   29065             :     "OGRERR_NONE if no error occurs (even if nothing is done) or an error\n"
   29066             :     "code. \n"
   29067             :     ""},
   29068             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetLayerDefn", _wrap_Layer_GetLayerDefn, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29069             :     "Layer_GetLayerDefn(Layer self) -> FeatureDefn\n"
   29070             :     "\n"
   29071             :     "OGRFeatureDefnH\n"
   29072             :     "OGR_L_GetLayerDefn(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   29073             :     "\n"
   29074             :     "Fetch the schema information for this layer.\n"
   29075             :     "\n"
   29076             :     "The returned handle to the OGRFeatureDefn is owned by the OGRLayer,\n"
   29077             :     "and should not be modified or freed by the application. It\n"
   29078             :     "encapsulates the attribute schema of the features of the layer.\n"
   29079             :     "\n"
   29080             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::GetLayerDefn().\n"
   29081             :     "\n"
   29082             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29083             :     "-----------\n"
   29084             :     "\n"
   29085             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer to get the schema information.\n"
   29086             :     "\n"
   29087             :     "an handle to the feature definition. \n"
   29088             :     ""},
   29089             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetFeatureCount", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_GetFeatureCount, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29090             :     "Layer_GetFeatureCount(Layer self, int force=1) -> GIntBig\n"
   29091             :     "\n"
   29092             :     "GIntBig\n"
   29093             :     "OGR_L_GetFeatureCount(OGRLayerH hLayer, int bForce)\n"
   29094             :     "\n"
   29095             :     "Fetch the feature count in this layer.\n"
   29096             :     "\n"
   29097             :     "Returns the number of features in the layer. For dynamic databases the\n"
   29098             :     "count may not be exact. If bForce is FALSE, and it would be expensive\n"
   29099             :     "to establish the feature count a value of -1 may be returned\n"
   29100             :     "indicating that the count isn't know. If bForce is TRUE some\n"
   29101             :     "implementations will actually scan the entire layer once to count\n"
   29102             :     "objects.\n"
   29103             :     "\n"
   29104             :     "The returned count takes the spatial filter into account.\n"
   29105             :     "\n"
   29106             :     "Note that some implementations of this method may alter the read\n"
   29107             :     "cursor of the layer.\n"
   29108             :     "\n"
   29109             :     "This function is the same as the CPP OGRLayer::GetFeatureCount().\n"
   29110             :     "\n"
   29111             :     "Note: since GDAL 2.0, this method returns a GIntBig (previously a int)\n"
   29112             :     "\n"
   29113             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29114             :     "-----------\n"
   29115             :     "\n"
   29116             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer that owned the features.\n"
   29117             :     "\n"
   29118             :     "bForce:  Flag indicating whether the count should be computed even if\n"
   29119             :     "it is expensive.\n"
   29120             :     "\n"
   29121             :     "feature count, -1 if count not known. \n"
   29122             :     ""},
   29123             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetExtent", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_GetExtent, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29124             :     "Layer_GetExtent(Layer self, int force=1, int can_return_null=0, int geom_field=0)\n"
   29125             :     "\n"
   29126             :     "OGRErr OGR_L_GetExtent(OGRLayerH\n"
   29127             :     "hLayer, OGREnvelope *psExtent, int bForce)\n"
   29128             :     "\n"
   29129             :     "Fetch the extent of this layer.\n"
   29130             :     "\n"
   29131             :     "Returns the extent (MBR) of the data in the layer. If bForce is FALSE,\n"
   29132             :     "and it would be expensive to establish the extent then OGRERR_FAILURE\n"
   29133             :     "will be returned indicating that the extent isn't know. If bForce is\n"
   29134             :     "TRUE then some implementations will actually scan the entire layer\n"
   29135             :     "once to compute the MBR of all the features in the layer.\n"
   29136             :     "\n"
   29137             :     "Depending on the drivers, the returned extent may or may not take the\n"
   29138             :     "spatial filter into account. So it is safer to call OGR_L_GetExtent()\n"
   29139             :     "without setting a spatial filter.\n"
   29140             :     "\n"
   29141             :     "Layers without any geometry may return OGRERR_FAILURE just indicating\n"
   29142             :     "that no meaningful extents could be collected.\n"
   29143             :     "\n"
   29144             :     "Note that some implementations of this method may alter the read\n"
   29145             :     "cursor of the layer.\n"
   29146             :     "\n"
   29147             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::GetExtent().\n"
   29148             :     "\n"
   29149             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29150             :     "-----------\n"
   29151             :     "\n"
   29152             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer from which to get extent.\n"
   29153             :     "\n"
   29154             :     "psExtent:  the structure in which the extent value will be returned.\n"
   29155             :     "\n"
   29156             :     "bForce:  Flag indicating whether the extent should be computed even if\n"
   29157             :     "it is expensive.\n"
   29158             :     "\n"
   29159             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success, OGRERR_FAILURE if extent not known. \n"
   29160             :     ""},
   29161             :    { (char *)"Layer_TestCapability", _wrap_Layer_TestCapability, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29162             :     "Layer_TestCapability(Layer self, char const * cap) -> bool\n"
   29163             :     "\n"
   29164             :     "int\n"
   29165             :     "OGR_L_TestCapability(OGRLayerH hLayer, const char *pszCap)\n"
   29166             :     "\n"
   29167             :     "Test if this layer supported the named capability.\n"
   29168             :     "\n"
   29169             :     "The capability codes that can be tested are represented as strings,\n"
   29170             :     "but #defined constants exists to ensure correct spelling. Specific\n"
   29171             :     "layer types may implement class specific capabilities, but this can't\n"
   29172             :     "generally be discovered by the caller.\n"
   29173             :     "\n"
   29174             :     "OLCRandomRead / \"RandomRead\": TRUE if the GetFeature() method is\n"
   29175             :     "implemented in an optimized way for this layer, as opposed to the\n"
   29176             :     "default implementation using ResetReading() and GetNextFeature() to\n"
   29177             :     "find the requested feature id.\n"
   29178             :     "\n"
   29179             :     "OLCSequentialWrite / \"SequentialWrite\": TRUE if the CreateFeature()\n"
   29180             :     "method works for this layer. Note this means that this particular\n"
   29181             :     "layer is writable. The same OGRLayer class may returned FALSE for\n"
   29182             :     "other layer instances that are effectively read-only.\n"
   29183             :     "\n"
   29184             :     "OLCRandomWrite / \"RandomWrite\": TRUE if the SetFeature() method is\n"
   29185             :     "operational on this layer. Note this means that this particular layer\n"
   29186             :     "is writable. The same OGRLayer class may returned FALSE for other\n"
   29187             :     "layer instances that are effectively read-only.\n"
   29188             :     "\n"
   29189             :     "OLCFastSpatialFilter / \"FastSpatialFilter\": TRUE if this layer\n"
   29190             :     "implements spatial filtering efficiently. Layers that effectively read\n"
   29191             :     "all features, and test them with the OGRFeature intersection methods\n"
   29192             :     "should return FALSE. This can be used as a clue by the application\n"
   29193             :     "whether it should build and maintain its own spatial index for\n"
   29194             :     "features in this layer.\n"
   29195             :     "\n"
   29196             :     "OLCFastFeatureCount / \"FastFeatureCount\": TRUE if this layer can\n"
   29197             :     "return a feature count (via OGR_L_GetFeatureCount()) efficiently, i.e.\n"
   29198             :     "without counting the features. In some cases this will return TRUE\n"
   29199             :     "until a spatial filter is installed after which it will return FALSE.\n"
   29200             :     "\n"
   29201             :     "OLCFastGetExtent / \"FastGetExtent\": TRUE if this layer can return\n"
   29202             :     "its data extent (via OGR_L_GetExtent()) efficiently, i.e. without\n"
   29203             :     "scanning all the features. In some cases this will return TRUE until a\n"
   29204             :     "spatial filter is installed after which it will return FALSE.\n"
   29205             :     "\n"
   29206             :     "OLCFastSetNextByIndex / \"FastSetNextByIndex\": TRUE if this layer can\n"
   29207             :     "perform the SetNextByIndex() call efficiently, otherwise FALSE.\n"
   29208             :     "\n"
   29209             :     "OLCCreateField / \"CreateField\": TRUE if this layer can create new\n"
   29210             :     "fields on the current layer using CreateField(), otherwise FALSE.\n"
   29211             :     "\n"
   29212             :     "OLCCreateGeomField / \"CreateGeomField\": (GDAL >= 1.11) TRUE if this\n"
   29213             :     "layer can create new geometry fields on the current layer using\n"
   29214             :     "CreateGeomField(), otherwise FALSE.\n"
   29215             :     "\n"
   29216             :     "OLCDeleteField / \"DeleteField\": TRUE if this layer can delete\n"
   29217             :     "existing fields on the current layer using DeleteField(), otherwise\n"
   29218             :     "FALSE.\n"
   29219             :     "\n"
   29220             :     "OLCReorderFields / \"ReorderFields\": TRUE if this layer can reorder\n"
   29221             :     "existing fields on the current layer using ReorderField() or\n"
   29222             :     "ReorderFields(), otherwise FALSE.\n"
   29223             :     "\n"
   29224             :     "OLCAlterFieldDefn / \"AlterFieldDefn\": TRUE if this layer can alter\n"
   29225             :     "the definition of an existing field on the current layer using\n"
   29226             :     "AlterFieldDefn(), otherwise FALSE.\n"
   29227             :     "\n"
   29228             :     "OLCDeleteFeature / \"DeleteFeature\": TRUE if the DeleteFeature()\n"
   29229             :     "method is supported on this layer, otherwise FALSE.\n"
   29230             :     "\n"
   29231             :     "OLCStringsAsUTF8 / \"StringsAsUTF8\": TRUE if values of OFTString\n"
   29232             :     "fields are assured to be in UTF-8 format. If FALSE the encoding of\n"
   29233             :     "fields is uncertain, though it might still be UTF-8.\n"
   29234             :     "\n"
   29235             :     "OLCTransactions / \"Transactions\": TRUE if the StartTransaction(),\n"
   29236             :     "CommitTransaction() and RollbackTransaction() methods work in a\n"
   29237             :     "meaningful way, otherwise FALSE.\n"
   29238             :     "\n"
   29239             :     "OLCCurveGeometries / \"CurveGeometries\": TRUE if this layer supports\n"
   29240             :     "writing curve geometries or may return such geometries. (GDAL 2.0).\n"
   29241             :     "\n"
   29242             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   29243             :     "OGRLayer::TestCapability().\n"
   29244             :     "\n"
   29245             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29246             :     "-----------\n"
   29247             :     "\n"
   29248             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer to get the capability from.\n"
   29249             :     "\n"
   29250             :     "pszCap:  the name of the capability to test.\n"
   29251             :     "\n"
   29252             :     "TRUE if the layer has the requested capability, or FALSE otherwise.\n"
   29253             :     "OGRLayers will return FALSE for any unrecognized capabilities. \n"
   29254             :     ""},
   29255             :    { (char *)"Layer_CreateField", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_CreateField, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29256             :     "Layer_CreateField(Layer self, FieldDefn field_def, int approx_ok=1) -> OGRErr\n"
   29257             :     "\n"
   29258             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29259             :     "OGR_L_CreateField(OGRLayerH hLayer, OGRFieldDefnH hField, int\n"
   29260             :     "bApproxOK)\n"
   29261             :     "\n"
   29262             :     "Create a new field on a layer.\n"
   29263             :     "\n"
   29264             :     "You must use this to create new fields on a real layer. Internally the\n"
   29265             :     "OGRFeatureDefn for the layer will be updated to reflect the new field.\n"
   29266             :     "Applications should never modify the OGRFeatureDefn used by a layer\n"
   29267             :     "directly.\n"
   29268             :     "\n"
   29269             :     "This function should not be called while there are feature objects in\n"
   29270             :     "existence that were obtained or created with the previous layer\n"
   29271             :     "definition.\n"
   29272             :     "\n"
   29273             :     "Not all drivers support this function. You can query a layer to check\n"
   29274             :     "if it supports it with the OLCCreateField capability. Some drivers may\n"
   29275             :     "only support this method while there are still no features in the\n"
   29276             :     "layer. When it is supported, the existing features of the backing\n"
   29277             :     "file/database should be updated accordingly.\n"
   29278             :     "\n"
   29279             :     "Drivers may or may not support not-null constraints. If they support\n"
   29280             :     "creating fields with not-null constraints, this is generally before\n"
   29281             :     "creating any feature to the layer.\n"
   29282             :     "\n"
   29283             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::CreateField().\n"
   29284             :     "\n"
   29285             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29286             :     "-----------\n"
   29287             :     "\n"
   29288             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer to write the field definition.\n"
   29289             :     "\n"
   29290             :     "hField:  handle of the field definition to write to disk.\n"
   29291             :     "\n"
   29292             :     "bApproxOK:  If TRUE, the field may be created in a slightly different\n"
   29293             :     "form depending on the limitations of the format driver.\n"
   29294             :     "\n"
   29295             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success. \n"
   29296             :     ""},
   29297             :    { (char *)"Layer_DeleteField", _wrap_Layer_DeleteField, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29298             :     "Layer_DeleteField(Layer self, int iField) -> OGRErr\n"
   29299             :     "\n"
   29300             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29301             :     "OGR_L_DeleteField(OGRLayerH hLayer, int iField)\n"
   29302             :     "\n"
   29303             :     "Delete an existing field on a layer.\n"
   29304             :     "\n"
   29305             :     "You must use this to delete existing fields on a real layer.\n"
   29306             :     "Internally the OGRFeatureDefn for the layer will be updated to reflect\n"
   29307             :     "the deleted field. Applications should never modify the OGRFeatureDefn\n"
   29308             :     "used by a layer directly.\n"
   29309             :     "\n"
   29310             :     "This function should not be called while there are feature objects in\n"
   29311             :     "existence that were obtained or created with the previous layer\n"
   29312             :     "definition.\n"
   29313             :     "\n"
   29314             :     "Not all drivers support this function. You can query a layer to check\n"
   29315             :     "if it supports it with the OLCDeleteField capability. Some drivers may\n"
   29316             :     "only support this method while there are still no features in the\n"
   29317             :     "layer. When it is supported, the existing features of the backing\n"
   29318             :     "file/database should be updated accordingly.\n"
   29319             :     "\n"
   29320             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::DeleteField().\n"
   29321             :     "\n"
   29322             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29323             :     "-----------\n"
   29324             :     "\n"
   29325             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer.\n"
   29326             :     "\n"
   29327             :     "iField:  index of the field to delete.\n"
   29328             :     "\n"
   29329             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success.\n"
   29330             :     "\n"
   29331             :     "OGR 1.9.0 \n"
   29332             :     ""},
   29333             :    { (char *)"Layer_ReorderField", _wrap_Layer_ReorderField, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29334             :     "Layer_ReorderField(Layer self, int iOldFieldPos, int iNewFieldPos) -> OGRErr\n"
   29335             :     "\n"
   29336             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29337             :     "OGR_L_ReorderField(OGRLayerH hLayer, int iOldFieldPos, int\n"
   29338             :     "iNewFieldPos)\n"
   29339             :     "\n"
   29340             :     "Reorder an existing field on a layer.\n"
   29341             :     "\n"
   29342             :     "This function is a convenience wrapper of OGR_L_ReorderFields()\n"
   29343             :     "dedicated to move a single field.\n"
   29344             :     "\n"
   29345             :     "You must use this to reorder existing fields on a real layer.\n"
   29346             :     "Internally the OGRFeatureDefn for the layer will be updated to reflect\n"
   29347             :     "the reordering of the fields. Applications should never modify the\n"
   29348             :     "OGRFeatureDefn used by a layer directly.\n"
   29349             :     "\n"
   29350             :     "This function should not be called while there are feature objects in\n"
   29351             :     "existence that were obtained or created with the previous layer\n"
   29352             :     "definition.\n"
   29353             :     "\n"
   29354             :     "The field definition that was at initial position iOldFieldPos will be\n"
   29355             :     "moved at position iNewFieldPos, and elements between will be shuffled\n"
   29356             :     "accordingly.\n"
   29357             :     "\n"
   29358             :     "For example, let suppose the fields were \"0\",\"1\",\"2\",\"3\",\"4\"\n"
   29359             :     "initially. ReorderField(1, 3) will reorder them as\n"
   29360             :     "\"0\",\"2\",\"3\",\"1\",\"4\".\n"
   29361             :     "\n"
   29362             :     "Not all drivers support this function. You can query a layer to check\n"
   29363             :     "if it supports it with the OLCReorderFields capability. Some drivers\n"
   29364             :     "may only support this method while there are still no features in the\n"
   29365             :     "layer. When it is supported, the existing features of the backing\n"
   29366             :     "file/database should be updated accordingly.\n"
   29367             :     "\n"
   29368             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::ReorderField().\n"
   29369             :     "\n"
   29370             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29371             :     "-----------\n"
   29372             :     "\n"
   29373             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer.\n"
   29374             :     "\n"
   29375             :     "iOldFieldPos:  previous position of the field to move. Must be in the\n"
   29376             :     "range [0,GetFieldCount()-1].\n"
   29377             :     "\n"
   29378             :     "iNewFieldPos:  new position of the field to move. Must be in the range\n"
   29379             :     "[0,GetFieldCount()-1].\n"
   29380             :     "\n"
   29381             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success.\n"
   29382             :     "\n"
   29383             :     "OGR 1.9.0 \n"
   29384             :     ""},
   29385             :    { (char *)"Layer_ReorderFields", _wrap_Layer_ReorderFields, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29386             :     "Layer_ReorderFields(Layer self, int nList) -> OGRErr\n"
   29387             :     "\n"
   29388             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29389             :     "OGR_L_ReorderFields(OGRLayerH hLayer, int *panMap)\n"
   29390             :     "\n"
   29391             :     "Reorder all the fields of a layer.\n"
   29392             :     "\n"
   29393             :     "You must use this to reorder existing fields on a real layer.\n"
   29394             :     "Internally the OGRFeatureDefn for the layer will be updated to reflect\n"
   29395             :     "the reordering of the fields. Applications should never modify the\n"
   29396             :     "OGRFeatureDefn used by a layer directly.\n"
   29397             :     "\n"
   29398             :     "This function should not be called while there are feature objects in\n"
   29399             :     "existence that were obtained or created with the previous layer\n"
   29400             :     "definition.\n"
   29401             :     "\n"
   29402             :     "panMap is such that,for each field definition at position i after\n"
   29403             :     "reordering, its position before reordering was panMap[i].\n"
   29404             :     "\n"
   29405             :     "For example, let suppose the fields were \"0\",\"1\",\"2\",\"3\",\"4\"\n"
   29406             :     "initially. ReorderFields([0,2,3,1,4]) will reorder them as\n"
   29407             :     "\"0\",\"2\",\"3\",\"1\",\"4\".\n"
   29408             :     "\n"
   29409             :     "Not all drivers support this function. You can query a layer to check\n"
   29410             :     "if it supports it with the OLCReorderFields capability. Some drivers\n"
   29411             :     "may only support this method while there are still no features in the\n"
   29412             :     "layer. When it is supported, the existing features of the backing\n"
   29413             :     "file/database should be updated accordingly.\n"
   29414             :     "\n"
   29415             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::ReorderFields().\n"
   29416             :     "\n"
   29417             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29418             :     "-----------\n"
   29419             :     "\n"
   29420             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer.\n"
   29421             :     "\n"
   29422             :     "panMap:  an array of GetLayerDefn()-> OGRFeatureDefn::GetFieldCount()\n"
   29423             :     "elements which is a permutation of [0, GetLayerDefn()->\n"
   29424             :     "OGRFeatureDefn::GetFieldCount()-1].\n"
   29425             :     "\n"
   29426             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success.\n"
   29427             :     "\n"
   29428             :     "OGR 1.9.0 \n"
   29429             :     ""},
   29430             :    { (char *)"Layer_AlterFieldDefn", _wrap_Layer_AlterFieldDefn, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29431             :     "Layer_AlterFieldDefn(Layer self, int iField, FieldDefn field_def, int nFlags) -> OGRErr\n"
   29432             :     "\n"
   29433             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29434             :     "OGR_L_AlterFieldDefn(OGRLayerH hLayer, int iField, OGRFieldDefnH\n"
   29435             :     "hNewFieldDefn, int nFlags)\n"
   29436             :     "\n"
   29437             :     "Alter the definition of an existing field on a layer.\n"
   29438             :     "\n"
   29439             :     "You must use this to alter the definition of an existing field of a\n"
   29440             :     "real layer. Internally the OGRFeatureDefn for the layer will be\n"
   29441             :     "updated to reflect the altered field. Applications should never modify\n"
   29442             :     "the OGRFeatureDefn used by a layer directly.\n"
   29443             :     "\n"
   29444             :     "This function should not be called while there are feature objects in\n"
   29445             :     "existence that were obtained or created with the previous layer\n"
   29446             :     "definition.\n"
   29447             :     "\n"
   29448             :     "Not all drivers support this function. You can query a layer to check\n"
   29449             :     "if it supports it with the OLCAlterFieldDefn capability. Some drivers\n"
   29450             :     "may only support this method while there are still no features in the\n"
   29451             :     "layer. When it is supported, the existing features of the backing\n"
   29452             :     "file/database should be updated accordingly. Some drivers might also\n"
   29453             :     "not support all update flags.\n"
   29454             :     "\n"
   29455             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   29456             :     "OGRLayer::AlterFieldDefn().\n"
   29457             :     "\n"
   29458             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29459             :     "-----------\n"
   29460             :     "\n"
   29461             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer.\n"
   29462             :     "\n"
   29463             :     "iField:  index of the field whose definition must be altered.\n"
   29464             :     "\n"
   29465             :     "hNewFieldDefn:  new field definition\n"
   29466             :     "\n"
   29467             :     "nFlags:  combination of ALTER_NAME_FLAG, ALTER_TYPE_FLAG,\n"
   29468             :     "ALTER_WIDTH_PRECISION_FLAG, ALTER_NULLABLE_FLAG and ALTER_DEFAULT_FLAG\n"
   29469             :     "to indicate which of the name and/or type and/or width and precision\n"
   29470             :     "fields and/or nullability from the new field definition must be taken\n"
   29471             :     "into account.\n"
   29472             :     "\n"
   29473             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success.\n"
   29474             :     "\n"
   29475             :     "OGR 1.9.0 \n"
   29476             :     ""},
   29477             :    { (char *)"Layer_CreateGeomField", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_CreateGeomField, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29478             :     "Layer_CreateGeomField(Layer self, GeomFieldDefn field_def, int approx_ok=1) -> OGRErr\n"
   29479             :     "\n"
   29480             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29481             :     "OGR_L_CreateGeomField(OGRLayerH hLayer, OGRGeomFieldDefnH hField, int\n"
   29482             :     "bApproxOK)\n"
   29483             :     "\n"
   29484             :     "Create a new geometry field on a layer.\n"
   29485             :     "\n"
   29486             :     "You must use this to create new geometry fields on a real layer.\n"
   29487             :     "Internally the OGRFeatureDefn for the layer will be updated to reflect\n"
   29488             :     "the new field. Applications should never modify the OGRFeatureDefn\n"
   29489             :     "used by a layer directly.\n"
   29490             :     "\n"
   29491             :     "This function should not be called while there are feature objects in\n"
   29492             :     "existence that were obtained or created with the previous layer\n"
   29493             :     "definition.\n"
   29494             :     "\n"
   29495             :     "Not all drivers support this function. You can query a layer to check\n"
   29496             :     "if it supports it with the OLCCreateField capability. Some drivers may\n"
   29497             :     "only support this method while there are still no features in the\n"
   29498             :     "layer. When it is supported, the existing features of the backing\n"
   29499             :     "file/database should be updated accordingly.\n"
   29500             :     "\n"
   29501             :     "Drivers may or may not support not-null constraints. If they support\n"
   29502             :     "creating fields with not-null constraints, this is generally before\n"
   29503             :     "creating any feature to the layer.\n"
   29504             :     "\n"
   29505             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::CreateField().\n"
   29506             :     "\n"
   29507             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29508             :     "-----------\n"
   29509             :     "\n"
   29510             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer to write the field definition.\n"
   29511             :     "\n"
   29512             :     "hField:  handle of the geometry field definition to write to disk.\n"
   29513             :     "\n"
   29514             :     "bApproxOK:  If TRUE, the field may be created in a slightly different\n"
   29515             :     "form depending on the limitations of the format driver.\n"
   29516             :     "\n"
   29517             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success.\n"
   29518             :     "\n"
   29519             :     "OGR 1.11 \n"
   29520             :     ""},
   29521             :    { (char *)"Layer_StartTransaction", _wrap_Layer_StartTransaction, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29522             :     "Layer_StartTransaction(Layer self) -> OGRErr\n"
   29523             :     "\n"
   29524             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29525             :     "OGR_L_StartTransaction(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   29526             :     "\n"
   29527             :     "For datasources which support transactions, StartTransaction creates a\n"
   29528             :     "transaction.\n"
   29529             :     "\n"
   29530             :     "If starting the transaction fails, will return OGRERR_FAILURE.\n"
   29531             :     "Datasources which do not support transactions will always return\n"
   29532             :     "OGRERR_NONE.\n"
   29533             :     "\n"
   29534             :     "Note: as of GDAL 2.0, use of this API is discouraged when the dataset\n"
   29535             :     "offers dataset level transaction with GDALDataset::StartTransaction().\n"
   29536             :     "The reason is that most drivers can only offer transactions at dataset\n"
   29537             :     "level, and not layer level. Very few drivers really support\n"
   29538             :     "transactions at layer scope.\n"
   29539             :     "\n"
   29540             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   29541             :     "OGRLayer::StartTransaction().\n"
   29542             :     "\n"
   29543             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29544             :     "-----------\n"
   29545             :     "\n"
   29546             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer\n"
   29547             :     "\n"
   29548             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success. \n"
   29549             :     ""},
   29550             :    { (char *)"Layer_CommitTransaction", _wrap_Layer_CommitTransaction, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29551             :     "Layer_CommitTransaction(Layer self) -> OGRErr\n"
   29552             :     "\n"
   29553             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29554             :     "OGR_L_CommitTransaction(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   29555             :     "\n"
   29556             :     "For datasources which support transactions, CommitTransaction commits\n"
   29557             :     "a transaction.\n"
   29558             :     "\n"
   29559             :     "If no transaction is active, or the commit fails, will return\n"
   29560             :     "OGRERR_FAILURE. Datasources which do not support transactions will\n"
   29561             :     "always return OGRERR_NONE.\n"
   29562             :     "\n"
   29563             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   29564             :     "OGRLayer::CommitTransaction().\n"
   29565             :     "\n"
   29566             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29567             :     "-----------\n"
   29568             :     "\n"
   29569             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer\n"
   29570             :     "\n"
   29571             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success. \n"
   29572             :     ""},
   29573             :    { (char *)"Layer_RollbackTransaction", _wrap_Layer_RollbackTransaction, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29574             :     "Layer_RollbackTransaction(Layer self) -> OGRErr\n"
   29575             :     "\n"
   29576             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29577             :     "OGR_L_RollbackTransaction(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   29578             :     "\n"
   29579             :     "For datasources which support transactions, RollbackTransaction will\n"
   29580             :     "roll back a datasource to its state before the start of the current\n"
   29581             :     "transaction.\n"
   29582             :     "\n"
   29583             :     "If no transaction is active, or the rollback fails, will return\n"
   29584             :     "OGRERR_FAILURE. Datasources which do not support transactions will\n"
   29585             :     "always return OGRERR_NONE.\n"
   29586             :     "\n"
   29587             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   29588             :     "OGRLayer::RollbackTransaction().\n"
   29589             :     "\n"
   29590             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29591             :     "-----------\n"
   29592             :     "\n"
   29593             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer\n"
   29594             :     "\n"
   29595             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success. \n"
   29596             :     ""},
   29597             :    { (char *)"Layer_FindFieldIndex", _wrap_Layer_FindFieldIndex, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29598             :     "Layer_FindFieldIndex(Layer self, char const * pszFieldName, int bExactMatch) -> int\n"
   29599             :     "\n"
   29600             :     "int\n"
   29601             :     "OGR_L_FindFieldIndex(OGRLayerH hLayer, const char *pszFieldName, int\n"
   29602             :     "bExactMatch)\n"
   29603             :     "\n"
   29604             :     "Find the index of field in a layer.\n"
   29605             :     "\n"
   29606             :     "The returned number is the index of the field in the layers, or -1 if\n"
   29607             :     "the field doesn't exist.\n"
   29608             :     "\n"
   29609             :     "If bExactMatch is set to FALSE and the field doesn't exists in the\n"
   29610             :     "given form the driver might apply some changes to make it match, like\n"
   29611             :     "those it might do if the layer was created (eg. like LAUNDER in the\n"
   29612             :     "OCI driver).\n"
   29613             :     "\n"
   29614             :     "This method is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::FindFieldIndex().\n"
   29615             :     "\n"
   29616             :     "field index, or -1 if the field doesn't exist \n"
   29617             :     ""},
   29618             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetSpatialRef", _wrap_Layer_GetSpatialRef, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29619             :     "Layer_GetSpatialRef(Layer self) -> SpatialReference\n"
   29620             :     "\n"
   29621             :     "OGRSpatialReferenceH\n"
   29622             :     "OGR_L_GetSpatialRef(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   29623             :     "\n"
   29624             :     "Fetch the spatial reference system for this layer.\n"
   29625             :     "\n"
   29626             :     "The returned object is owned by the OGRLayer and should not be\n"
   29627             :     "modified or freed by the application.\n"
   29628             :     "\n"
   29629             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::GetSpatialRef().\n"
   29630             :     "\n"
   29631             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29632             :     "-----------\n"
   29633             :     "\n"
   29634             :     "hLayer:  handle to the layer to get the spatial reference from.\n"
   29635             :     "\n"
   29636             :     "spatial reference, or NULL if there isn't one. \n"
   29637             :     ""},
   29638             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetFeaturesRead", _wrap_Layer_GetFeaturesRead, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29639             :     "Layer_GetFeaturesRead(Layer self) -> GIntBig\n"
   29640             :     "\n"
   29641             :     "GIntBig\n"
   29642             :     "OGR_L_GetFeaturesRead(OGRLayerH hLayer) \n"
   29643             :     ""},
   29644             :    { (char *)"Layer_SetIgnoredFields", _wrap_Layer_SetIgnoredFields, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   29645             :     "Layer_SetIgnoredFields(Layer self, char const ** options) -> OGRErr\n"
   29646             :     "\n"
   29647             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29648             :     "OGR_L_SetIgnoredFields(OGRLayerH hLayer, const char **papszFields)\n"
   29649             :     "\n"
   29650             :     "Set which fields can be omitted when retrieving features from the\n"
   29651             :     "layer.\n"
   29652             :     "\n"
   29653             :     "If the driver supports this functionality (testable using\n"
   29654             :     "OLCIgnoreFields capability), it will not fetch the specified fields in\n"
   29655             :     "subsequent calls to GetFeature() / GetNextFeature() and thus save some\n"
   29656             :     "processing time and/or bandwidth.\n"
   29657             :     "\n"
   29658             :     "Besides field names of the layers, the following special fields can be\n"
   29659             :     "passed: \"OGR_GEOMETRY\" to ignore geometry and \"OGR_STYLE\" to\n"
   29660             :     "ignore layer style.\n"
   29661             :     "\n"
   29662             :     "By default, no fields are ignored.\n"
   29663             :     "\n"
   29664             :     "This method is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::SetIgnoredFields()\n"
   29665             :     "\n"
   29666             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29667             :     "-----------\n"
   29668             :     "\n"
   29669             :     "papszFields:  an array of field names terminated by NULL item. If NULL\n"
   29670             :     "is passed, the ignored list is cleared.\n"
   29671             :     "\n"
   29672             :     "OGRERR_NONE if all field names have been resolved (even if the driver\n"
   29673             :     "does not support this method) \n"
   29674             :     ""},
   29675             :    { (char *)"Layer_Intersection", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_Intersection, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29676             :     "Layer_Intersection(Layer self, Layer method_layer, Layer result_layer, char ** options=None, GDALProgressFunc callback=0, void * callback_data=None) -> OGRErr\n"
   29677             :     "\n"
   29678             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29679             :     "OGR_L_Intersection(OGRLayerH pLayerInput, OGRLayerH pLayerMethod,\n"
   29680             :     "OGRLayerH pLayerResult, char **papszOptions, GDALProgressFunc\n"
   29681             :     "pfnProgress, void *pProgressArg)\n"
   29682             :     "\n"
   29683             :     "Intersection of two layers.\n"
   29684             :     "\n"
   29685             :     "The result layer contains features whose geometries represent areas\n"
   29686             :     "that are common between features in the input layer and in the method\n"
   29687             :     "layer. The features in the result layer have attributes from both\n"
   29688             :     "input and method layers. The schema of the result layer can be set by\n"
   29689             :     "the user or, if it is empty, is initialized to contain all fields in\n"
   29690             :     "the input and method layers.\n"
   29691             :     "\n"
   29692             :     "If the schema of the result is set by user and contains fields that\n"
   29693             :     "have the same name as a field in input and in method layer, then the\n"
   29694             :     "attribute in the result feature will get the value from the feature of\n"
   29695             :     "the method layer.\n"
   29696             :     "\n"
   29697             :     "For best performance use the minimum amount of features in the method\n"
   29698             :     "layer and copy it into a memory layer.\n"
   29699             :     "\n"
   29700             :     "This method relies on GEOS support. Do not use unless the GEOS support\n"
   29701             :     "is compiled in.  The recognized list of options is :\n"
   29702             :     "SKIP_FAILURES=YES/NO. Set it to YES to go on, even when a feature\n"
   29703             :     "could not be inserted or a GEOS call failed.\n"
   29704             :     "\n"
   29705             :     "PROMOTE_TO_MULTI=YES/NO. Set it to YES to convert Polygons into\n"
   29706             :     "MultiPolygons, or LineStrings to MultiLineStrings.\n"
   29707             :     "\n"
   29708             :     "INPUT_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   29709             :     "created from the fields of the input layer.\n"
   29710             :     "\n"
   29711             :     "METHOD_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   29712             :     "created from the fields of the method layer.\n"
   29713             :     "\n"
   29714             :     "USE_PREPARED_GEOMETRIES=YES/NO. Set to NO to not use prepared\n"
   29715             :     "geometries to pretest intersection of features of method layer with\n"
   29716             :     "features of this layer.\n"
   29717             :     "\n"
   29718             :     "PRETEST_CONTAINMENT=YES/NO. Set to YES to pretest the containment of\n"
   29719             :     "features of method layer within the features of this layer. This will\n"
   29720             :     "speed up the method significantly in some cases. Requires that the\n"
   29721             :     "prepared geometries are in effect.\n"
   29722             :     "\n"
   29723             :     "KEEP_LOWER_DIMENSION_GEOMETRIES=YES/NO. Set to NO to skip result\n"
   29724             :     "features with lower dimension geometry that would otherwise be added\n"
   29725             :     "to the result layer. The default is to add but only if the result\n"
   29726             :     "layer has an unknown geometry type.\n"
   29727             :     "\n"
   29728             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::Intersection().\n"
   29729             :     "\n"
   29730             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29731             :     "-----------\n"
   29732             :     "\n"
   29733             :     "pLayerInput:  the input layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   29734             :     "\n"
   29735             :     "pLayerMethod:  the method layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   29736             :     "\n"
   29737             :     "pLayerResult:  the layer where the features resulting from the\n"
   29738             :     "operation are inserted. Should not be NULL. See above the note about\n"
   29739             :     "the schema.\n"
   29740             :     "\n"
   29741             :     "papszOptions:  NULL terminated list of options (may be NULL).\n"
   29742             :     "\n"
   29743             :     "pfnProgress:  a GDALProgressFunc() compatible callback function for\n"
   29744             :     "reporting progress or NULL.\n"
   29745             :     "\n"
   29746             :     "pProgressArg:  argument to be passed to pfnProgress. May be NULL.\n"
   29747             :     "\n"
   29748             :     "an error code if there was an error or the execution was interrupted,\n"
   29749             :     "OGRERR_NONE otherwise.\n"
   29750             :     "\n"
   29751             :     "The first geometry field is always used.\n"
   29752             :     "\n"
   29753             :     "OGR 1.10 \n"
   29754             :     ""},
   29755             :    { (char *)"Layer_Union", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_Union, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29756             :     "Layer_Union(Layer self, Layer method_layer, Layer result_layer, char ** options=None, GDALProgressFunc callback=0, void * callback_data=None) -> OGRErr\n"
   29757             :     "\n"
   29758             :     "OGRErr OGR_L_Union(OGRLayerH\n"
   29759             :     "pLayerInput, OGRLayerH pLayerMethod, OGRLayerH pLayerResult, char\n"
   29760             :     "**papszOptions, GDALProgressFunc pfnProgress, void *pProgressArg)\n"
   29761             :     "\n"
   29762             :     "Union of two layers.\n"
   29763             :     "\n"
   29764             :     "The result layer contains features whose geometries represent areas\n"
   29765             :     "that are in either in the input layer, in the method layer, or in\n"
   29766             :     "both. The features in the result layer have attributes from both input\n"
   29767             :     "and method layers. For features which represent areas that are only in\n"
   29768             :     "the input or in the method layer the respective attributes have\n"
   29769             :     "undefined values. The schema of the result layer can be set by the\n"
   29770             :     "user or, if it is empty, is initialized to contain all fields in the\n"
   29771             :     "input and method layers.\n"
   29772             :     "\n"
   29773             :     "If the schema of the result is set by user and contains fields that\n"
   29774             :     "have the same name as a field in input and in method layer, then the\n"
   29775             :     "attribute in the result feature will get the value from the feature of\n"
   29776             :     "the method layer (even if it is undefined).\n"
   29777             :     "\n"
   29778             :     "For best performance use the minimum amount of features in the method\n"
   29779             :     "layer and copy it into a memory layer.\n"
   29780             :     "\n"
   29781             :     "This method relies on GEOS support. Do not use unless the GEOS support\n"
   29782             :     "is compiled in.  The recognized list of options is :\n"
   29783             :     "SKIP_FAILURES=YES/NO. Set it to YES to go on, even when a feature\n"
   29784             :     "could not be inserted or a GEOS call failed.\n"
   29785             :     "\n"
   29786             :     "PROMOTE_TO_MULTI=YES/NO. Set it to YES to convert Polygons into\n"
   29787             :     "MultiPolygons, or LineStrings to MultiLineStrings.\n"
   29788             :     "\n"
   29789             :     "INPUT_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   29790             :     "created from the fields of the input layer.\n"
   29791             :     "\n"
   29792             :     "METHOD_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   29793             :     "created from the fields of the method layer.\n"
   29794             :     "\n"
   29795             :     "USE_PREPARED_GEOMETRIES=YES/NO. Set to NO to not use prepared\n"
   29796             :     "geometries to pretest intersection of features of method layer with\n"
   29797             :     "features of this layer.\n"
   29798             :     "\n"
   29799             :     "KEEP_LOWER_DIMENSION_GEOMETRIES=YES/NO. Set to NO to skip result\n"
   29800             :     "features with lower dimension geometry that would otherwise be added\n"
   29801             :     "to the result layer. The default is to add but only if the result\n"
   29802             :     "layer has an unknown geometry type.\n"
   29803             :     "\n"
   29804             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::Union().\n"
   29805             :     "\n"
   29806             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29807             :     "-----------\n"
   29808             :     "\n"
   29809             :     "pLayerInput:  the input layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   29810             :     "\n"
   29811             :     "pLayerMethod:  the method layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   29812             :     "\n"
   29813             :     "pLayerResult:  the layer where the features resulting from the\n"
   29814             :     "operation are inserted. Should not be NULL. See above the note about\n"
   29815             :     "the schema.\n"
   29816             :     "\n"
   29817             :     "papszOptions:  NULL terminated list of options (may be NULL).\n"
   29818             :     "\n"
   29819             :     "pfnProgress:  a GDALProgressFunc() compatible callback function for\n"
   29820             :     "reporting progress or NULL.\n"
   29821             :     "\n"
   29822             :     "pProgressArg:  argument to be passed to pfnProgress. May be NULL.\n"
   29823             :     "\n"
   29824             :     "an error code if there was an error or the execution was interrupted,\n"
   29825             :     "OGRERR_NONE otherwise.\n"
   29826             :     "\n"
   29827             :     "The first geometry field is always used.\n"
   29828             :     "\n"
   29829             :     "OGR 1.10 \n"
   29830             :     ""},
   29831             :    { (char *)"Layer_SymDifference", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_SymDifference, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29832             :     "Layer_SymDifference(Layer self, Layer method_layer, Layer result_layer, char ** options=None, GDALProgressFunc callback=0, void * callback_data=None) -> OGRErr\n"
   29833             :     "\n"
   29834             :     "OGRErr\n"
   29835             :     "OGR_L_SymDifference(OGRLayerH pLayerInput, OGRLayerH pLayerMethod,\n"
   29836             :     "OGRLayerH pLayerResult, char **papszOptions, GDALProgressFunc\n"
   29837             :     "pfnProgress, void *pProgressArg)\n"
   29838             :     "\n"
   29839             :     "Symmetrical difference of two layers.\n"
   29840             :     "\n"
   29841             :     "The result layer contains features whose geometries represent areas\n"
   29842             :     "that are in either in the input layer or in the method layer but not\n"
   29843             :     "in both. The features in the result layer have attributes from both\n"
   29844             :     "input and method layers. For features which represent areas that are\n"
   29845             :     "only in the input or in the method layer the respective attributes\n"
   29846             :     "have undefined values. The schema of the result layer can be set by\n"
   29847             :     "the user or, if it is empty, is initialized to contain all fields in\n"
   29848             :     "the input and method layers.\n"
   29849             :     "\n"
   29850             :     "If the schema of the result is set by user and contains fields that\n"
   29851             :     "have the same name as a field in input and in method layer, then the\n"
   29852             :     "attribute in the result feature will get the value from the feature of\n"
   29853             :     "the method layer (even if it is undefined).\n"
   29854             :     "\n"
   29855             :     "For best performance use the minimum amount of features in the method\n"
   29856             :     "layer and copy it into a memory layer.\n"
   29857             :     "\n"
   29858             :     "This method relies on GEOS support. Do not use unless the GEOS support\n"
   29859             :     "is compiled in.  The recognized list of options is :\n"
   29860             :     "SKIP_FAILURES=YES/NO. Set it to YES to go on, even when a feature\n"
   29861             :     "could not be inserted or a GEOS call failed.\n"
   29862             :     "\n"
   29863             :     "PROMOTE_TO_MULTI=YES/NO. Set it to YES to convert Polygons into\n"
   29864             :     "MultiPolygons, or LineStrings to MultiLineStrings.\n"
   29865             :     "\n"
   29866             :     "INPUT_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   29867             :     "created from the fields of the input layer.\n"
   29868             :     "\n"
   29869             :     "METHOD_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   29870             :     "created from the fields of the method layer.\n"
   29871             :     "\n"
   29872             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::SymDifference().\n"
   29873             :     "\n"
   29874             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29875             :     "-----------\n"
   29876             :     "\n"
   29877             :     "pLayerInput:  the input layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   29878             :     "\n"
   29879             :     "pLayerMethod:  the method layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   29880             :     "\n"
   29881             :     "pLayerResult:  the layer where the features resulting from the\n"
   29882             :     "operation are inserted. Should not be NULL. See above the note about\n"
   29883             :     "the schema.\n"
   29884             :     "\n"
   29885             :     "papszOptions:  NULL terminated list of options (may be NULL).\n"
   29886             :     "\n"
   29887             :     "pfnProgress:  a GDALProgressFunc() compatible callback function for\n"
   29888             :     "reporting progress or NULL.\n"
   29889             :     "\n"
   29890             :     "pProgressArg:  argument to be passed to pfnProgress. May be NULL.\n"
   29891             :     "\n"
   29892             :     "an error code if there was an error or the execution was interrupted,\n"
   29893             :     "OGRERR_NONE otherwise.\n"
   29894             :     "\n"
   29895             :     "The first geometry field is always used.\n"
   29896             :     "\n"
   29897             :     "OGR 1.10 \n"
   29898             :     ""},
   29899             :    { (char *)"Layer_Identity", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_Identity, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29900             :     "Layer_Identity(Layer self, Layer method_layer, Layer result_layer, char ** options=None, GDALProgressFunc callback=0, void * callback_data=None) -> OGRErr\n"
   29901             :     "\n"
   29902             :     "OGRErr OGR_L_Identity(OGRLayerH\n"
   29903             :     "pLayerInput, OGRLayerH pLayerMethod, OGRLayerH pLayerResult, char\n"
   29904             :     "**papszOptions, GDALProgressFunc pfnProgress, void *pProgressArg)\n"
   29905             :     "\n"
   29906             :     "Identify the features of this layer with the ones from the identity\n"
   29907             :     "layer.\n"
   29908             :     "\n"
   29909             :     "The result layer contains features whose geometries represent areas\n"
   29910             :     "that are in the input layer. The features in the result layer have\n"
   29911             :     "attributes from both input and method layers. The schema of the result\n"
   29912             :     "layer can be set by the user or, if it is empty, is initialized to\n"
   29913             :     "contain all fields in input and method layers.\n"
   29914             :     "\n"
   29915             :     "If the schema of the result is set by user and contains fields that\n"
   29916             :     "have the same name as a field in input and in method layer, then the\n"
   29917             :     "attribute in the result feature will get the value from the feature of\n"
   29918             :     "the method layer (even if it is undefined).\n"
   29919             :     "\n"
   29920             :     "For best performance use the minimum amount of features in the method\n"
   29921             :     "layer and copy it into a memory layer.\n"
   29922             :     "\n"
   29923             :     "This method relies on GEOS support. Do not use unless the GEOS support\n"
   29924             :     "is compiled in.  The recognized list of options is :\n"
   29925             :     "SKIP_FAILURES=YES/NO. Set it to YES to go on, even when a feature\n"
   29926             :     "could not be inserted or a GEOS call failed.\n"
   29927             :     "\n"
   29928             :     "PROMOTE_TO_MULTI=YES/NO. Set it to YES to convert Polygons into\n"
   29929             :     "MultiPolygons, or LineStrings to MultiLineStrings.\n"
   29930             :     "\n"
   29931             :     "INPUT_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   29932             :     "created from the fields of the input layer.\n"
   29933             :     "\n"
   29934             :     "METHOD_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   29935             :     "created from the fields of the method layer.\n"
   29936             :     "\n"
   29937             :     "USE_PREPARED_GEOMETRIES=YES/NO. Set to NO to not use prepared\n"
   29938             :     "geometries to pretest intersection of features of method layer with\n"
   29939             :     "features of this layer.\n"
   29940             :     "\n"
   29941             :     "KEEP_LOWER_DIMENSION_GEOMETRIES=YES/NO. Set to NO to skip result\n"
   29942             :     "features with lower dimension geometry that would otherwise be added\n"
   29943             :     "to the result layer. The default is to add but only if the result\n"
   29944             :     "layer has an unknown geometry type.\n"
   29945             :     "\n"
   29946             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::Identity().\n"
   29947             :     "\n"
   29948             :     "Parameters:\n"
   29949             :     "-----------\n"
   29950             :     "\n"
   29951             :     "pLayerInput:  the input layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   29952             :     "\n"
   29953             :     "pLayerMethod:  the method layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   29954             :     "\n"
   29955             :     "pLayerResult:  the layer where the features resulting from the\n"
   29956             :     "operation are inserted. Should not be NULL. See above the note about\n"
   29957             :     "the schema.\n"
   29958             :     "\n"
   29959             :     "papszOptions:  NULL terminated list of options (may be NULL).\n"
   29960             :     "\n"
   29961             :     "pfnProgress:  a GDALProgressFunc() compatible callback function for\n"
   29962             :     "reporting progress or NULL.\n"
   29963             :     "\n"
   29964             :     "pProgressArg:  argument to be passed to pfnProgress. May be NULL.\n"
   29965             :     "\n"
   29966             :     "an error code if there was an error or the execution was interrupted,\n"
   29967             :     "OGRERR_NONE otherwise.\n"
   29968             :     "\n"
   29969             :     "The first geometry field is always used.\n"
   29970             :     "\n"
   29971             :     "OGR 1.10 \n"
   29972             :     ""},
   29973             :    { (char *)"Layer_Update", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_Update, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   29974             :     "Layer_Update(Layer self, Layer method_layer, Layer result_layer, char ** options=None, GDALProgressFunc callback=0, void * callback_data=None) -> OGRErr\n"
   29975             :     "\n"
   29976             :     "OGRErr OGR_L_Update(OGRLayerH\n"
   29977             :     "pLayerInput, OGRLayerH pLayerMethod, OGRLayerH pLayerResult, char\n"
   29978             :     "**papszOptions, GDALProgressFunc pfnProgress, void *pProgressArg)\n"
   29979             :     "\n"
   29980             :     "Update this layer with features from the update layer.\n"
   29981             :     "\n"
   29982             :     "The result layer contains features whose geometries represent areas\n"
   29983             :     "that are either in the input layer or in the method layer. The\n"
   29984             :     "features in the result layer have areas of the features of the method\n"
   29985             :     "layer or those ares of the features of the input layer that are not\n"
   29986             :     "covered by the method layer. The features of the result layer get\n"
   29987             :     "their attributes from the input layer. The schema of the result layer\n"
   29988             :     "can be set by the user or, if it is empty, is initialized to contain\n"
   29989             :     "all fields in the input layer.\n"
   29990             :     "\n"
   29991             :     "If the schema of the result is set by user and contains fields that\n"
   29992             :     "have the same name as a field in the method layer, then the attribute\n"
   29993             :     "in the result feature the originates from the method layer will get\n"
   29994             :     "the value from the feature of the method layer.\n"
   29995             :     "\n"
   29996             :     "For best performance use the minimum amount of features in the method\n"
   29997             :     "layer and copy it into a memory layer.\n"
   29998             :     "\n"
   29999             :     "This method relies on GEOS support. Do not use unless the GEOS support\n"
   30000             :     "is compiled in.  The recognized list of options is :\n"
   30001             :     "SKIP_FAILURES=YES/NO. Set it to YES to go on, even when a feature\n"
   30002             :     "could not be inserted or a GEOS call failed.\n"
   30003             :     "\n"
   30004             :     "PROMOTE_TO_MULTI=YES/NO. Set it to YES to convert Polygons into\n"
   30005             :     "MultiPolygons, or LineStrings to MultiLineStrings.\n"
   30006             :     "\n"
   30007             :     "INPUT_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   30008             :     "created from the fields of the input layer.\n"
   30009             :     "\n"
   30010             :     "METHOD_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   30011             :     "created from the fields of the method layer.\n"
   30012             :     "\n"
   30013             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::Update().\n"
   30014             :     "\n"
   30015             :     "Parameters:\n"
   30016             :     "-----------\n"
   30017             :     "\n"
   30018             :     "pLayerInput:  the input layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   30019             :     "\n"
   30020             :     "pLayerMethod:  the method layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   30021             :     "\n"
   30022             :     "pLayerResult:  the layer where the features resulting from the\n"
   30023             :     "operation are inserted. Should not be NULL. See above the note about\n"
   30024             :     "the schema.\n"
   30025             :     "\n"
   30026             :     "papszOptions:  NULL terminated list of options (may be NULL).\n"
   30027             :     "\n"
   30028             :     "pfnProgress:  a GDALProgressFunc() compatible callback function for\n"
   30029             :     "reporting progress or NULL.\n"
   30030             :     "\n"
   30031             :     "pProgressArg:  argument to be passed to pfnProgress. May be NULL.\n"
   30032             :     "\n"
   30033             :     "an error code if there was an error or the execution was interrupted,\n"
   30034             :     "OGRERR_NONE otherwise.\n"
   30035             :     "\n"
   30036             :     "The first geometry field is always used.\n"
   30037             :     "\n"
   30038             :     "OGR 1.10 \n"
   30039             :     ""},
   30040             :    { (char *)"Layer_Clip", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_Clip, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   30041             :     "Layer_Clip(Layer self, Layer method_layer, Layer result_layer, char ** options=None, GDALProgressFunc callback=0, void * callback_data=None) -> OGRErr\n"
   30042             :     "\n"
   30043             :     "OGRErr OGR_L_Clip(OGRLayerH pLayerInput,\n"
   30044             :     "OGRLayerH pLayerMethod, OGRLayerH pLayerResult, char **papszOptions,\n"
   30045             :     "GDALProgressFunc pfnProgress, void *pProgressArg)\n"
   30046             :     "\n"
   30047             :     "Clip off areas that are not covered by the method layer.\n"
   30048             :     "\n"
   30049             :     "The result layer contains features whose geometries represent areas\n"
   30050             :     "that are in the input layer and in the method layer. The features in\n"
   30051             :     "the result layer have the (possibly clipped) areas of features in the\n"
   30052             :     "input layer and the attributes from the same features. The schema of\n"
   30053             :     "the result layer can be set by the user or, if it is empty, is\n"
   30054             :     "initialized to contain all fields in the input layer.\n"
   30055             :     "\n"
   30056             :     "For best performance use the minimum amount of features in the method\n"
   30057             :     "layer and copy it into a memory layer.\n"
   30058             :     "\n"
   30059             :     "This method relies on GEOS support. Do not use unless the GEOS support\n"
   30060             :     "is compiled in.  The recognized list of options is :\n"
   30061             :     "SKIP_FAILURES=YES/NO. Set it to YES to go on, even when a feature\n"
   30062             :     "could not be inserted or a GEOS call failed.\n"
   30063             :     "\n"
   30064             :     "PROMOTE_TO_MULTI=YES/NO. Set it to YES to convert Polygons into\n"
   30065             :     "MultiPolygons, or LineStrings to MultiLineStrings.\n"
   30066             :     "\n"
   30067             :     "INPUT_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   30068             :     "created from the fields of the input layer.\n"
   30069             :     "\n"
   30070             :     "METHOD_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   30071             :     "created from the fields of the method layer.\n"
   30072             :     "\n"
   30073             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::Clip().\n"
   30074             :     "\n"
   30075             :     "Parameters:\n"
   30076             :     "-----------\n"
   30077             :     "\n"
   30078             :     "pLayerInput:  the input layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   30079             :     "\n"
   30080             :     "pLayerMethod:  the method layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   30081             :     "\n"
   30082             :     "pLayerResult:  the layer where the features resulting from the\n"
   30083             :     "operation are inserted. Should not be NULL. See above the note about\n"
   30084             :     "the schema.\n"
   30085             :     "\n"
   30086             :     "papszOptions:  NULL terminated list of options (may be NULL).\n"
   30087             :     "\n"
   30088             :     "pfnProgress:  a GDALProgressFunc() compatible callback function for\n"
   30089             :     "reporting progress or NULL.\n"
   30090             :     "\n"
   30091             :     "pProgressArg:  argument to be passed to pfnProgress. May be NULL.\n"
   30092             :     "\n"
   30093             :     "an error code if there was an error or the execution was interrupted,\n"
   30094             :     "OGRERR_NONE otherwise.\n"
   30095             :     "\n"
   30096             :     "The first geometry field is always used.\n"
   30097             :     "\n"
   30098             :     "OGR 1.10 \n"
   30099             :     ""},
   30100             :    { (char *)"Layer_Erase", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Layer_Erase, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"\n"
   30101             :     "Layer_Erase(Layer self, Layer method_layer, Layer result_layer, char ** options=None, GDALProgressFunc callback=0, void * callback_data=None) -> OGRErr\n"
   30102             :     "\n"
   30103             :     "OGRErr OGR_L_Erase(OGRLayerH\n"
   30104             :     "pLayerInput, OGRLayerH pLayerMethod, OGRLayerH pLayerResult, char\n"
   30105             :     "**papszOptions, GDALProgressFunc pfnProgress, void *pProgressArg)\n"
   30106             :     "\n"
   30107             :     "Remove areas that are covered by the method layer.\n"
   30108             :     "\n"
   30109             :     "The result layer contains features whose geometries represent areas\n"
   30110             :     "that are in the input layer but not in the method layer. The features\n"
   30111             :     "in the result layer have attributes from the input layer. The schema\n"
   30112             :     "of the result layer can be set by the user or, if it is empty, is\n"
   30113             :     "initialized to contain all fields in the input layer.\n"
   30114             :     "\n"
   30115             :     "For best performance use the minimum amount of features in the method\n"
   30116             :     "layer and copy it into a memory layer.\n"
   30117             :     "\n"
   30118             :     "This method relies on GEOS support. Do not use unless the GEOS support\n"
   30119             :     "is compiled in.  The recognized list of options is :\n"
   30120             :     "SKIP_FAILURES=YES/NO. Set it to YES to go on, even when a feature\n"
   30121             :     "could not be inserted or a GEOS call failed.\n"
   30122             :     "\n"
   30123             :     "PROMOTE_TO_MULTI=YES/NO. Set it to YES to convert Polygons into\n"
   30124             :     "MultiPolygons, or LineStrings to MultiLineStrings.\n"
   30125             :     "\n"
   30126             :     "INPUT_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   30127             :     "created from the fields of the input layer.\n"
   30128             :     "\n"
   30129             :     "METHOD_PREFIX=string. Set a prefix for the field names that will be\n"
   30130             :     "created from the fields of the method layer.\n"
   30131             :     "\n"
   30132             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRLayer::Erase().\n"
   30133             :     "\n"
   30134             :     "Parameters:\n"
   30135             :     "-----------\n"
   30136             :     "\n"
   30137             :     "pLayerInput:  the input layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   30138             :     "\n"
   30139             :     "pLayerMethod:  the method layer. Should not be NULL.\n"
   30140             :     "\n"
   30141             :     "pLayerResult:  the layer where the features resulting from the\n"
   30142             :     "operation are inserted. Should not be NULL. See above the note about\n"
   30143             :     "the schema.\n"
   30144             :     "\n"
   30145             :     "papszOptions:  NULL terminated list of options (may be NULL).\n"
   30146             :     "\n"
   30147             :     "pfnProgress:  a GDALProgressFunc() compatible callback function for\n"
   30148             :     "reporting progress or NULL.\n"
   30149             :     "\n"
   30150             :     "pProgressArg:  argument to be passed to pfnProgress. May be NULL.\n"
   30151             :     "\n"
   30152             :     "an error code if there was an error or the execution was interrupted,\n"
   30153             :     "OGRERR_NONE otherwise.\n"
   30154             :     "\n"
   30155             :     "The first geometry field is always used.\n"
   30156             :     "\n"
   30157             :     "OGR 1.10 \n"
   30158             :     ""},
   30159             :    { (char *)"Layer_GetStyleTable", _wrap_Layer_GetStyleTable, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   30160             :     "Layer_GetStyleTable(Layer self) -> StyleTable\n"
   30161             :     "\n"
   30162             :     "OGRStyleTableH\n"
   30163             :     "OGR_L_GetStyleTable(OGRLayerH hLayer)\n"
   30164             :     "\n"
   30165             :     "Get style table. \n"
   30166             :     ""},
   30167             :    { (char *)"Layer_SetStyleTable", _wrap_Layer_SetStyleTable, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   30168             :     "Layer_SetStyleTable(Layer self, StyleTable table)\n"
   30169             :     "\n"
   30170             :     "void\n"
   30171             :     "OGR_L_SetStyleTable(OGRLayerH hLayer, OGRStyleTableH hStyleTable)\n"
   30172             :     "\n"
   30173             :     "Set style table. \n"
   30174             :     ""},
   30175             :    { (char *)"Layer_swigregister", Layer_swigregister, METH_VARARGS, NULL},
   30176             :    { (char *)"delete_Feature", _wrap_delete_Feature, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"delete_Feature(Feature self)"},
   30177             :    { (char *)"new_Feature", (PyCFunction) _wrap_new_Feature, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"new_Feature(FeatureDefn feature_def) -> Feature"},
   30178             :    { (char *)"Feature_GetDefnRef", _wrap_Feature_GetDefnRef, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   30179             :     "Feature_GetDefnRef(Feature self) -> FeatureDefn\n"
   30180             :     "\n"
   30181             :     "OGRFeatureDefnH\n"
   30182             :     "OGR_F_GetDefnRef(OGRFeatureH hFeat)\n"
   30183             :     "\n"
   30184             :     "Fetch feature definition.\n"
   30185             :     "\n"
   30186             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRFeature::GetDefnRef().\n"
   30187             :     "\n"
   30188             :     "Parameters:\n"
   30189             :     "-----------\n"
   30190             :     "\n"
   30191             :     "hFeat:  handle to the feature to get the feature definition from.\n"
   30192             :     "\n"
   30193             :     "an handle to the feature definition object on which feature depends.\n"
   30194             :     "\n"
   30195             :     ""},
   30196             :    { (char *)"Feature_SetGeometry", _wrap_Feature_SetGeometry, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   30197             :     "Feature_SetGeometry(Feature self, Geometry geom) -> OGRErr\n"
   30198             :     "\n"
   30199             :     "OGRErr\n"
   30200             :     "OGR_F_SetGeometry(OGRFeatureH hFeat, OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   30201             :     "\n"
   30202             :     "Set feature geometry.\n"
   30203             :     "\n"
   30204             :     "This function updates the features geometry, and operate exactly as\n"
   30205             :     "SetGeometryDirectly(), except that this function does not assume\n"
   30206             :     "ownership of the passed geometry, but instead makes a copy of it.\n"
   30207             :     "\n"
   30208             :     "This function is the same as the C++ OGRFeature::SetGeometry().\n"
   30209             :     "\n"
   30210             :     "This method has only an effect on the in-memory feature object. If\n"
   30211             :     "this object comes from a layer and the modifications must be\n"
   30212             :     "serialized back to the datasource, OGR_L_SetFeature() must be used\n"
   30213             :     "afterwards. Or if this is a new feature, OGR_L_CreateFeature() must be\n"
   30214             :     "used afterwards.\n"
   30215             :     "\n"
   30216             :     "Parameters:\n"
   30217             :     "-----------\n"
   30218             :     "\n"
   30219             :     "hFeat:  handle to the feature on which new geometry is applied to.\n"
   30220             :     "\n"
   30221             :     "hGeom:  handle to the new geometry to apply to feature.\n"
   30222             :     "\n"
   30223             :     "OGRERR_NONE if successful, or OGR_UNSUPPORTED_GEOMETRY_TYPE if the\n"
   30224             :     "geometry type is illegal for the OGRFeatureDefn (checking not yet\n"
   30225             :     "implemented). \n"
   30226             :     ""},
