   32780             :     "Geometry_Buffer(Geometry self, double distance, int quadsecs=30) -> Geometry\n"
   32781             :     "\n"
   32782             :     "OGRGeometryH OGR_G_Buffer(OGRGeometryH\n"
   32783             :     "hTarget, double dfDist, int nQuadSegs)\n"
   32784             :     "\n"
   32785             :     "Compute buffer of geometry.\n"
   32786             :     "\n"
   32787             :     "Builds a new geometry containing the buffer region around the geometry\n"
   32788             :     "on which it is invoked. The buffer is a polygon containing the region\n"
   32789             :     "within the buffer distance of the original geometry.\n"
   32790             :     "\n"
   32791             :     "Some buffer sections are properly described as curves, but are\n"
   32792             :     "converted to approximate polygons. The nQuadSegs parameter can be used\n"
   32793             :     "to control how many segments should be used to define a 90 degree\n"
   32794             :     "curve - a quadrant of a circle. A value of 30 is a reasonable default.\n"
   32795             :     "Large values result in large numbers of vertices in the resulting\n"
   32796             :     "buffer geometry while small numbers reduce the accuracy of the result.\n"
   32797             :     "\n"
   32798             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Buffer().\n"
   32799             :     "\n"
   32800             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   32801             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   32802             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   32803             :     "error.\n"
   32804             :     "\n"
   32805             :     "Parameters:\n"
   32806             :     "-----------\n"
   32807             :     "\n"
   32808             :     "hTarget:  the geometry.\n"
   32809             :     "\n"
   32810             :     "dfDist:  the buffer distance to be applied. Should be expressed into\n"
   32811             :     "the same unit as the coordinates of the geometry.\n"
   32812             :     "\n"
   32813             :     "nQuadSegs:  the number of segments used to approximate a 90 degree\n"
   32814             :     "(quadrant) of curvature.\n"
   32815             :     "\n"
   32816             :     "the newly created geometry, or NULL if an error occurs. \n"
   32817             :     ""},
   32818             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Intersection", _wrap_Geometry_Intersection, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   32819             :     "Geometry_Intersection(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> Geometry\n"
   32820             :     "\n"
   32821             :     "OGRGeometryH\n"
   32822             :     "OGR_G_Intersection(OGRGeometryH hThis, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   32823             :     "\n"
   32824             :     "Compute intersection.\n"
   32825             :     "\n"
   32826             :     "Generates a new geometry which is the region of intersection of the\n"
   32827             :     "two geometries operated on. The OGR_G_Intersects() function can be\n"
   32828             :     "used to test if two geometries intersect.\n"
   32829             :     "\n"
   32830             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   32831             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   32832             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   32833             :     "\n"
   32834             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   32835             :     "OGRGeometry::Intersection().\n"
   32836             :     "\n"
   32837             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   32838             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   32839             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   32840             :     "error.\n"
   32841             :     "\n"
   32842             :     "Parameters:\n"
   32843             :     "-----------\n"
   32844             :     "\n"
   32845             :     "hThis:  the geometry.\n"
   32846             :     "\n"
   32847             :     "hOther:  the other geometry.\n"
   32848             :     "\n"
   32849             :     "a new geometry representing the intersection or NULL if there is no\n"
   32850             :     "intersection or an error occurs. \n"
   32851             :     ""},
   32852             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Union", _wrap_Geometry_Union, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   32853             :     "Geometry_Union(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> Geometry\n"
   32854             :     "\n"
   32855             :     "OGRGeometryH OGR_G_Union(OGRGeometryH\n"
   32856             :     "hThis, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   32857             :     "\n"
   32858             :     "Compute union.\n"
   32859             :     "\n"
   32860             :     "Generates a new geometry which is the region of union of the two\n"
   32861             :     "geometries operated on.\n"
   32862             :     "\n"
   32863             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   32864             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   32865             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   32866             :     "\n"
   32867             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Union().\n"
   32868             :     "\n"
   32869             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   32870             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   32871             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   32872             :     "error.\n"
   32873             :     "\n"
   32874             :     "Parameters:\n"
   32875             :     "-----------\n"
   32876             :     "\n"
   32877             :     "hThis:  the geometry.\n"
   32878             :     "\n"
   32879             :     "hOther:  the other geometry.\n"
   32880             :     "\n"
   32881             :     "a new geometry representing the union or NULL if an error occurs. \n"
   32882             :     ""},
   32883             :    { (char *)"Geometry_UnionCascaded", _wrap_Geometry_UnionCascaded, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   32884             :     "Geometry_UnionCascaded(Geometry self) -> Geometry\n"
   32885             :     "\n"
   32886             :     "OGRGeometryH\n"
   32887             :     "OGR_G_UnionCascaded(OGRGeometryH hThis)\n"
   32888             :     "\n"
   32889             :     "Compute union using cascading.\n"
   32890             :     "\n"
   32891             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   32892             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   32893             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   32894             :     "\n"
   32895             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   32896             :     "OGRGeometry::UnionCascaded().\n"
   32897             :     "\n"
   32898             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   32899             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   32900             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   32901             :     "error.\n"
   32902             :     "\n"
   32903             :     "Parameters:\n"
   32904             :     "-----------\n"
   32905             :     "\n"
   32906             :     "hThis:  the geometry.\n"
   32907             :     "\n"
   32908             :     "a new geometry representing the union or NULL if an error occurs. \n"
   32909             :     ""},
   32910             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Difference", _wrap_Geometry_Difference, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   32911             :     "Geometry_Difference(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> Geometry\n"
   32912             :     "\n"
   32913             :     "OGRGeometryH\n"
   32914             :     "OGR_G_Difference(OGRGeometryH hThis, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   32915             :     "\n"
   32916             :     "Compute difference.\n"
   32917             :     "\n"
   32918             :     "Generates a new geometry which is the region of this geometry with the\n"
   32919             :     "region of the other geometry removed.\n"
   32920             :     "\n"
   32921             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   32922             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   32923             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   32924             :     "\n"
   32925             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Difference().\n"
   32926             :     "\n"
   32927             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   32928             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   32929             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   32930             :     "error.\n"
   32931             :     "\n"
   32932             :     "Parameters:\n"
   32933             :     "-----------\n"
   32934             :     "\n"
   32935             :     "hThis:  the geometry.\n"
   32936             :     "\n"
   32937             :     "hOther:  the other geometry.\n"
   32938             :     "\n"
   32939             :     "a new geometry representing the difference or NULL if the difference\n"
   32940             :     "is empty or an error occurs. \n"
   32941             :     ""},
   32942             :    { (char *)"Geometry_SymDifference", _wrap_Geometry_SymDifference, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   32943             :     "Geometry_SymDifference(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> Geometry\n"
   32944             :     "\n"
   32945             :     "OGRGeometryH\n"
   32946             :     "OGR_G_SymDifference(OGRGeometryH hThis, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   32947             :     "\n"
   32948             :     "Compute symmetric difference.\n"
   32949             :     "\n"
   32950             :     "Generates a new geometry which is the symmetric difference of this\n"
   32951             :     "geometry and the other geometry.\n"
   32952             :     "\n"
   32953             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   32954             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   32955             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   32956             :     "\n"
   32957             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method\n"
   32958             :     "OGRGeometry::SymmetricDifference().\n"
   32959             :     "\n"
   32960             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   32961             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   32962             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   32963             :     "error.\n"
   32964             :     "\n"
   32965             :     "Parameters:\n"
   32966             :     "-----------\n"
   32967             :     "\n"
   32968             :     "hThis:  the geometry.\n"
   32969             :     "\n"
   32970             :     "hOther:  the other geometry.\n"
   32971             :     "\n"
   32972             :     "a new geometry representing the symmetric difference or NULL if the\n"
   32973             :     "difference is empty or an error occurs.\n"
   32974             :     "\n"
   32975             :     "OGR 1.8.0 \n"
   32976             :     ""},
   32977             :    { (char *)"Geometry_SymmetricDifference", _wrap_Geometry_SymmetricDifference, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   32978             :     "Geometry_SymmetricDifference(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> Geometry\n"
   32979             :     "\n"
   32980             :     "OGRGeometryH\n"
   32981             :     "OGR_G_SymmetricDifference(OGRGeometryH hThis, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   32982             :     "\n"
   32983             :     "Compute symmetric difference (deprecated)\n"
   32984             :     "\n"
   32985             :     "Deprecated\n"
   32986             :     "\n"
   32987             :     "See:  OGR_G_SymmetricDifference() \n"
   32988             :     ""},
   32989             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Distance", _wrap_Geometry_Distance, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   32990             :     "Geometry_Distance(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> double\n"
   32991             :     "\n"
   32992             :     "double OGR_G_Distance(OGRGeometryH\n"
   32993             :     "hFirst, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   32994             :     "\n"
   32995             :     "Compute distance between two geometries.\n"
   32996             :     "\n"
   32997             :     "Returns the shortest distance between the two geometries. The distance\n"
   32998             :     "is expressed into the same unit as the coordinates of the geometries.\n"
   32999             :     "\n"
   33000             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Distance().\n"
   33001             :     "\n"
   33002             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33003             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33004             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   33005             :     "error.\n"
   33006             :     "\n"
   33007             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33008             :     "-----------\n"
   33009             :     "\n"
   33010             :     "hFirst:  the first geometry to compare against.\n"
   33011             :     "\n"
   33012             :     "hOther:  the other geometry to compare against.\n"
   33013             :     "\n"
   33014             :     "the distance between the geometries or -1 if an error occurs. \n"
   33015             :     ""},
   33016             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Distance3D", _wrap_Geometry_Distance3D, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33017             :     "Geometry_Distance3D(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> double\n"
   33018             :     "\n"
   33019             :     "double\n"
   33020             :     "OGR_G_Distance3D(OGRGeometryH hFirst, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   33021             :     "\n"
   33022             :     "Returns the 3D distance between two geometries.\n"
   33023             :     "\n"
   33024             :     "The distance is expressed into the same unit as the coordinates of the\n"
   33025             :     "geometries.\n"
   33026             :     "\n"
   33027             :     "This method is built on the SFCGAL library, check it for the\n"
   33028             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the\n"
   33029             :     "SFCGAL library, this method will always return -1.0\n"
   33030             :     "\n"
   33031             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Distance3D().\n"
   33032             :     "\n"
   33033             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33034             :     "-----------\n"
   33035             :     "\n"
   33036             :     "hFirst:  the first geometry to compare against.\n"
   33037             :     "\n"
   33038             :     "hOther:  the other geometry to compare against.\n"
   33039             :     "\n"
   33040             :     "distance between the two geometries\n"
   33041             :     "\n"
   33042             :     "GDAL 2.2\n"
   33043             :     "\n"
   33044             :     "the distance between the geometries or -1 if an error occurs. \n"
   33045             :     ""},
   33046             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Empty", _wrap_Geometry_Empty, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33047             :     "Geometry_Empty(Geometry self)\n"
   33048             :     "\n"
   33049             :     "void OGR_G_Empty(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33050             :     "\n"
   33051             :     "Clear geometry information.\n"
   33052             :     "\n"
   33053             :     "This restores the geometry to its initial state after construction,\n"
   33054             :     "and before assignment of actual geometry.\n"
   33055             :     "\n"
   33056             :     "This function relates to the SFCOM IGeometry::Empty() method.\n"
   33057             :     "\n"
   33058             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::empty().\n"
   33059             :     "\n"
   33060             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33061             :     "-----------\n"
   33062             :     "\n"
   33063             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to empty. \n"
   33064             :     ""},
   33065             :    { (char *)"Geometry_IsEmpty", _wrap_Geometry_IsEmpty, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33066             :     "Geometry_IsEmpty(Geometry self) -> bool\n"
   33067             :     "\n"
   33068             :     "int OGR_G_IsEmpty(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33069             :     "\n"
   33070             :     "Test if the geometry is empty.\n"
   33071             :     "\n"
   33072             :     "This method is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::IsEmpty().\n"
   33073             :     "\n"
   33074             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33075             :     "-----------\n"
   33076             :     "\n"
   33077             :     "hGeom:  The Geometry to test.\n"
   33078             :     "\n"
   33079             :     "TRUE if the geometry has no points, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33080             :     ""},
   33081             :    { (char *)"Geometry_IsValid", _wrap_Geometry_IsValid, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33082             :     "Geometry_IsValid(Geometry self) -> bool\n"
   33083             :     "\n"
   33084             :     "int OGR_G_IsValid(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33085             :     "\n"
   33086             :     "Test if the geometry is valid.\n"
   33087             :     "\n"
   33088             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::IsValid().\n"
   33089             :     "\n"
   33090             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33091             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33092             :     "library, this function will always return FALSE.\n"
   33093             :     "\n"
   33094             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33095             :     "-----------\n"
   33096             :     "\n"
   33097             :     "hGeom:  The Geometry to test.\n"
   33098             :     "\n"
   33099             :     "TRUE if the geometry has no points, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33100             :     ""},
   33101             :    { (char *)"Geometry_IsSimple", _wrap_Geometry_IsSimple, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33102             :     "Geometry_IsSimple(Geometry self) -> bool\n"
   33103             :     "\n"
   33104             :     "int OGR_G_IsSimple(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33105             :     "hGeom)\n"
   33106             :     "\n"
   33107             :     "Returns TRUE if the geometry is simple.\n"
   33108             :     "\n"
   33109             :     "Returns TRUE if the geometry has no anomalous geometric points, such\n"
   33110             :     "as self intersection or self tangency. The description of each\n"
   33111             :     "instantiable geometric class will include the specific conditions that\n"
   33112             :     "cause an instance of that class to be classified as not simple.\n"
   33113             :     "\n"
   33114             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::IsSimple()\n"
   33115             :     "method.\n"
   33116             :     "\n"
   33117             :     "If OGR is built without the GEOS library, this function will always\n"
   33118             :     "return FALSE.\n"
   33119             :     "\n"
   33120             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33121             :     "-----------\n"
   33122             :     "\n"
   33123             :     "hGeom:  The Geometry to test.\n"
   33124             :     "\n"
   33125             :     "TRUE if object is simple, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33126             :     ""},
   33127             :    { (char *)"Geometry_IsRing", _wrap_Geometry_IsRing, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33128             :     "Geometry_IsRing(Geometry self) -> bool\n"
   33129             :     "\n"
   33130             :     "int OGR_G_IsRing(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33131             :     "\n"
   33132             :     "Test if the geometry is a ring.\n"
   33133             :     "\n"
   33134             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::IsRing().\n"
   33135             :     "\n"
   33136             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33137             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33138             :     "library, this function will always return FALSE.\n"
   33139             :     "\n"
   33140             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33141             :     "-----------\n"
   33142             :     "\n"
   33143             :     "hGeom:  The Geometry to test.\n"
   33144             :     "\n"
   33145             :     "TRUE if the geometry has no points, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33146             :     ""},
   33147             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Intersects", _wrap_Geometry_Intersects, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33148             :     "Geometry_Intersects(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool\n"
   33149             :     "\n"
   33150             :     "int OGR_G_Intersects(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33151             :     "hGeom, OGRGeometryH hOtherGeom)\n"
   33152             :     "\n"
   33153             :     "Do these features intersect?\n"
   33154             :     "\n"
   33155             :     "Determines whether two geometries intersect. If GEOS is enabled, then\n"
   33156             :     "this is done in rigorous fashion otherwise TRUE is returned if the\n"
   33157             :     "envelopes (bounding boxes) of the two geometries overlap.\n"
   33158             :     "\n"
   33159             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::Intersects.\n"
   33160             :     "\n"
   33161             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33162             :     "-----------\n"
   33163             :     "\n"
   33164             :     "hGeom:  handle on the first geometry.\n"
   33165             :     "\n"
   33166             :     "hOtherGeom:  handle on the other geometry to test against.\n"
   33167             :     "\n"
   33168             :     "TRUE if the geometries intersect, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33169             :     ""},
   33170             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Intersect", _wrap_Geometry_Intersect, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"Geometry_Intersect(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool"},
   33171             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Equals", _wrap_Geometry_Equals, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33172             :     "Geometry_Equals(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool\n"
   33173             :     "\n"
   33174             :     "int OGR_G_Equals(OGRGeometryH hGeom,\n"
   33175             :     "OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   33176             :     "\n"
   33177             :     "Returns TRUE if two geometries are equivalent.\n"
   33178             :     "\n"
   33179             :     "This operation implements the SQL/MM ST_OrderingEquals() operation.\n"
   33180             :     "\n"
   33181             :     "The comparison is done in a structural way, that is to say that the\n"
   33182             :     "geometry types must be identical, as well as the number and ordering\n"
   33183             :     "of sub-geometries and vertices. Or equivalently, two geometries are\n"
   33184             :     "considered equal by this method if their WKT/WKB representation is\n"
   33185             :     "equal. Note: this must be distinguished for equality in a spatial way\n"
   33186             :     "(which is the purpose of the ST_Equals() operation).\n"
   33187             :     "\n"
   33188             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::Equals()\n"
   33189             :     "method.\n"
   33190             :     "\n"
   33191             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33192             :     "-----------\n"
   33193             :     "\n"
   33194             :     "hGeom:  handle on the first geometry.\n"
   33195             :     "\n"
   33196             :     "hOther:  handle on the other geometry to test against.\n"
   33197             :     "\n"
   33198             :     "TRUE if equivalent or FALSE otherwise. \n"
   33199             :     ""},
   33200             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Equal", _wrap_Geometry_Equal, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"Geometry_Equal(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool"},
   33201             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Disjoint", _wrap_Geometry_Disjoint, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33202             :     "Geometry_Disjoint(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool\n"
   33203             :     "\n"
   33204             :     "int OGR_G_Disjoint(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33205             :     "hThis, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   33206             :     "\n"
   33207             :     "Test for disjointness.\n"
   33208             :     "\n"
   33209             :     "Tests if this geometry and the other geometry are disjoint.\n"
   33210             :     "\n"
   33211             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   33212             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   33213             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   33214             :     "\n"
   33215             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Disjoint().\n"
   33216             :     "\n"
   33217             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33218             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33219             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   33220             :     "error.\n"
   33221             :     "\n"
   33222             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33223             :     "-----------\n"
   33224             :     "\n"
   33225             :     "hThis:  the geometry to compare.\n"
   33226             :     "\n"
   33227             :     "hOther:  the other geometry to compare.\n"
   33228             :     "\n"
   33229             :     "TRUE if they are disjoint, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33230             :     ""},
   33231             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Touches", _wrap_Geometry_Touches, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33232             :     "Geometry_Touches(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool\n"
   33233             :     "\n"
   33234             :     "int OGR_G_Touches(OGRGeometryH hThis,\n"
   33235             :     "OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   33236             :     "\n"
   33237             :     "Test for touching.\n"
   33238             :     "\n"
   33239             :     "Tests if this geometry and the other geometry are touching.\n"
   33240             :     "\n"
   33241             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   33242             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   33243             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   33244             :     "\n"
   33245             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Touches().\n"
   33246             :     "\n"
   33247             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33248             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33249             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   33250             :     "error.\n"
   33251             :     "\n"
   33252             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33253             :     "-----------\n"
   33254             :     "\n"
   33255             :     "hThis:  the geometry to compare.\n"
   33256             :     "\n"
   33257             :     "hOther:  the other geometry to compare.\n"
   33258             :     "\n"
   33259             :     "TRUE if they are touching, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33260             :     ""},
   33261             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Crosses", _wrap_Geometry_Crosses, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33262             :     "Geometry_Crosses(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool\n"
   33263             :     "\n"
   33264             :     "int OGR_G_Crosses(OGRGeometryH hThis,\n"
   33265             :     "OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   33266             :     "\n"
   33267             :     "Test for crossing.\n"
   33268             :     "\n"
   33269             :     "Tests if this geometry and the other geometry are crossing.\n"
   33270             :     "\n"
   33271             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   33272             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   33273             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   33274             :     "\n"
   33275             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Crosses().\n"
   33276             :     "\n"
   33277             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33278             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33279             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   33280             :     "error.\n"
   33281             :     "\n"
   33282             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33283             :     "-----------\n"
   33284             :     "\n"
   33285             :     "hThis:  the geometry to compare.\n"
   33286             :     "\n"
   33287             :     "hOther:  the other geometry to compare.\n"
   33288             :     "\n"
   33289             :     "TRUE if they are crossing, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33290             :     ""},
   33291             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Within", _wrap_Geometry_Within, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33292             :     "Geometry_Within(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool\n"
   33293             :     "\n"
   33294             :     "int OGR_G_Within(OGRGeometryH hThis,\n"
   33295             :     "OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   33296             :     "\n"
   33297             :     "Test for containment.\n"
   33298             :     "\n"
   33299             :     "Tests if this geometry is within the other geometry.\n"
   33300             :     "\n"
   33301             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   33302             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   33303             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   33304             :     "\n"
   33305             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Within().\n"
   33306             :     "\n"
   33307             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33308             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33309             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   33310             :     "error.\n"
   33311             :     "\n"
   33312             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33313             :     "-----------\n"
   33314             :     "\n"
   33315             :     "hThis:  the geometry to compare.\n"
   33316             :     "\n"
   33317             :     "hOther:  the other geometry to compare.\n"
   33318             :     "\n"
   33319             :     "TRUE if hThis is within hOther, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33320             :     ""},
   33321             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Contains", _wrap_Geometry_Contains, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33322             :     "Geometry_Contains(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool\n"
   33323             :     "\n"
   33324             :     "int OGR_G_Contains(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33325             :     "hThis, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   33326             :     "\n"
   33327             :     "Test for containment.\n"
   33328             :     "\n"
   33329             :     "Tests if this geometry contains the other geometry.\n"
   33330             :     "\n"
   33331             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   33332             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   33333             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   33334             :     "\n"
   33335             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Contains().\n"
   33336             :     "\n"
   33337             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33338             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33339             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   33340             :     "error.\n"
   33341             :     "\n"
   33342             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33343             :     "-----------\n"
   33344             :     "\n"
   33345             :     "hThis:  the geometry to compare.\n"
   33346             :     "\n"
   33347             :     "hOther:  the other geometry to compare.\n"
   33348             :     "\n"
   33349             :     "TRUE if hThis contains hOther geometry, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33350             :     ""},
   33351             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Overlaps", _wrap_Geometry_Overlaps, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33352             :     "Geometry_Overlaps(Geometry self, Geometry other) -> bool\n"
   33353             :     "\n"
   33354             :     "int OGR_G_Overlaps(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33355             :     "hThis, OGRGeometryH hOther)\n"
   33356             :     "\n"
   33357             :     "Test for overlap.\n"
   33358             :     "\n"
   33359             :     "Tests if this geometry and the other geometry overlap, that is their\n"
   33360             :     "intersection has a non-zero area.\n"
   33361             :     "\n"
   33362             :     "Geometry validity is not checked. In case you are unsure of the\n"
   33363             :     "validity of the input geometries, call IsValid() before, otherwise the\n"
   33364             :     "result might be wrong.\n"
   33365             :     "\n"
   33366             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Overlaps().\n"
   33367             :     "\n"
   33368             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33369             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33370             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   33371             :     "error.\n"
   33372             :     "\n"
   33373             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33374             :     "-----------\n"
   33375             :     "\n"
   33376             :     "hThis:  the geometry to compare.\n"
   33377             :     "\n"
   33378             :     "hOther:  the other geometry to compare.\n"
   33379             :     "\n"
   33380             :     "TRUE if they are overlapping, otherwise FALSE. \n"
   33381             :     ""},
   33382             :    { (char *)"Geometry_TransformTo", _wrap_Geometry_TransformTo, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33383             :     "Geometry_TransformTo(Geometry self, SpatialReference reference) -> OGRErr\n"
   33384             :     "\n"
   33385             :     "OGRErr\n"
   33386             :     "OGR_G_TransformTo(OGRGeometryH hGeom, OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS)\n"
   33387             :     "\n"
   33388             :     "Transform geometry to new spatial reference system.\n"
   33389             :     "\n"
   33390             :     "This function will transform the coordinates of a geometry from their\n"
   33391             :     "current spatial reference system to a new target spatial reference\n"
   33392             :     "system. Normally this means reprojecting the vectors, but it could\n"
   33393             :     "include datum shifts, and changes of units.\n"
   33394             :     "\n"
   33395             :     "This function will only work if the geometry already has an assigned\n"
   33396             :     "spatial reference system, and if it is transformable to the target\n"
   33397             :     "coordinate system.\n"
   33398             :     "\n"
   33399             :     "Because this function requires internal creation and initialization of\n"
   33400             :     "an OGRCoordinateTransformation object it is significantly more\n"
   33401             :     "expensive to use this function to transform many geometries than it is\n"
   33402             :     "to create the OGRCoordinateTransformation in advance, and call\n"
   33403             :     "transform() with that transformation. This function exists primarily\n"
   33404             :     "for convenience when only transforming a single geometry.\n"
   33405             :     "\n"
   33406             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::transformTo.\n"
   33407             :     "\n"
   33408             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33409             :     "-----------\n"
   33410             :     "\n"
   33411             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to apply the transform to.\n"
   33412             :     "\n"
   33413             :     "hSRS:  handle on the spatial reference system to apply.\n"
   33414             :     "\n"
   33415             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success, or an error code. \n"
   33416             :     ""},
   33417             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Transform", _wrap_Geometry_Transform, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33418             :     "Geometry_Transform(Geometry self, CoordinateTransformation trans) -> OGRErr\n"
   33419             :     "\n"
   33420             :     "OGRErr OGR_G_Transform(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33421             :     "hGeom, OGRCoordinateTransformationH hTransform)\n"
   33422             :     "\n"
   33423             :     "Apply arbitrary coordinate transformation to geometry.\n"
   33424             :     "\n"
   33425             :     "This function will transform the coordinates of a geometry from their\n"
   33426             :     "current spatial reference system to a new target spatial reference\n"
   33427             :     "system. Normally this means reprojecting the vectors, but it could\n"
   33428             :     "include datum shifts, and changes of units.\n"
   33429             :     "\n"
   33430             :     "Note that this function does not require that the geometry already\n"
   33431             :     "have a spatial reference system. It will be assumed that they can be\n"
   33432             :     "treated as having the source spatial reference system of the\n"
   33433             :     "OGRCoordinateTransformation object, and the actual SRS of the geometry\n"
   33434             :     "will be ignored. On successful completion the output\n"
   33435             :     "OGRSpatialReference of the OGRCoordinateTransformation will be\n"
   33436             :     "assigned to the geometry.\n"
   33437             :     "\n"
   33438             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::transform.\n"
   33439             :     "\n"
   33440             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33441             :     "-----------\n"
   33442             :     "\n"
   33443             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to apply the transform to.\n"
   33444             :     "\n"
   33445             :     "hTransform:  handle on the transformation to apply.\n"
   33446             :     "\n"
   33447             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success or an error code. \n"
   33448             :     ""},
   33449             :    { (char *)"Geometry_GetSpatialReference", _wrap_Geometry_GetSpatialReference, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33450             :     "Geometry_GetSpatialReference(Geometry self) -> SpatialReference\n"
   33451             :     "\n"
   33452             :     "OGRSpatialReferenceH\n"
   33453             :     "OGR_G_GetSpatialReference(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33454             :     "\n"
   33455             :     "Returns spatial reference system for geometry.\n"
   33456             :     "\n"
   33457             :     "This function relates to the SFCOM IGeometry::get_SpatialReference()\n"
   33458             :     "method.\n"
   33459             :     "\n"
   33460             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method\n"
   33461             :     "OGRGeometry::getSpatialReference().\n"
   33462             :     "\n"
   33463             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33464             :     "-----------\n"
   33465             :     "\n"
   33466             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to get spatial reference from.\n"
   33467             :     "\n"
   33468             :     "a reference to the spatial reference geometry. \n"
   33469             :     ""},
   33470             :    { (char *)"Geometry_AssignSpatialReference", _wrap_Geometry_AssignSpatialReference, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33471             :     "Geometry_AssignSpatialReference(Geometry self, SpatialReference reference)\n"
   33472             :     "\n"
   33473             :     "void\n"
   33474             :     "OGR_G_AssignSpatialReference(OGRGeometryH hGeom, OGRSpatialReferenceH\n"
   33475             :     "hSRS)\n"
   33476             :     "\n"
   33477             :     "Assign spatial reference to this object.\n"
   33478             :     "\n"
   33479             :     "Any existing spatial reference is replaced, but under no circumstances\n"
   33480             :     "does this result in the object being reprojected. It is just changing\n"
   33481             :     "the interpretation of the existing geometry. Note that assigning a\n"
   33482             :     "spatial reference increments the reference count on the\n"
   33483             :     "OGRSpatialReference, but does not copy it.\n"
   33484             :     "\n"
   33485             :     "Starting with GDAL 2.3, this will also assign the spatial reference to\n"
   33486             :     "potential sub-geometries of the geometry ( OGRGeometryCollection,\n"
   33487             :     "OGRCurvePolygon/OGRPolygon, OGRCompoundCurve, OGRPolyhedralSurface and\n"
   33488             :     "their derived classes).\n"
   33489             :     "\n"
   33490             :     "This is similar to the SFCOM IGeometry::put_SpatialReference() method.\n"
   33491             :     "\n"
   33492             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method\n"
   33493             :     "OGRGeometry::assignSpatialReference.\n"
   33494             :     "\n"
   33495             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33496             :     "-----------\n"
   33497             :     "\n"
   33498             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to apply the new spatial reference\n"
   33499             :     "system.\n"
   33500             :     "\n"
   33501             :     "hSRS:  handle on the new spatial reference system to apply. \n"
   33502             :     ""},
   33503             :    { (char *)"Geometry_CloseRings", _wrap_Geometry_CloseRings, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33504             :     "Geometry_CloseRings(Geometry self)\n"
   33505             :     "\n"
   33506             :     "void OGR_G_CloseRings(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33507             :     "hGeom)\n"
   33508             :     "\n"
   33509             :     "Force rings to be closed.\n"
   33510             :     "\n"
   33511             :     "If this geometry, or any contained geometries has polygon rings that\n"
   33512             :     "are not closed, they will be closed by adding the starting point at\n"
   33513             :     "the end.\n"
   33514             :     "\n"
   33515             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33516             :     "-----------\n"
   33517             :     "\n"
   33518             :     "hGeom:  handle to the geometry. \n"
   33519             :     ""},
   33520             :    { (char *)"Geometry_FlattenTo2D", _wrap_Geometry_FlattenTo2D, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33521             :     "Geometry_FlattenTo2D(Geometry self)\n"
   33522             :     "\n"
   33523             :     "void\n"
   33524             :     "OGR_G_FlattenTo2D(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33525             :     "\n"
   33526             :     "Convert geometry to strictly 2D.\n"
   33527             :     "\n"
   33528             :     "In a sense this converts all Z coordinates to 0.0.\n"
   33529             :     "\n"
   33530             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method\n"
   33531             :     "OGRGeometry::flattenTo2D().\n"
   33532             :     "\n"
   33533             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33534             :     "-----------\n"
   33535             :     "\n"
   33536             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to convert. \n"
   33537             :     ""},
   33538             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Segmentize", _wrap_Geometry_Segmentize, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33539             :     "Geometry_Segmentize(Geometry self, double dfMaxLength)\n"
   33540             :     "\n"
   33541             :     "void OGR_G_Segmentize(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33542             :     "hGeom, double dfMaxLength)\n"
   33543             :     "\n"
   33544             :     "Modify the geometry such it has no segment longer then the given\n"
   33545             :     "distance.\n"
   33546             :     "\n"
   33547             :     "Interpolated points will have Z and M values (if needed) set to 0.\n"
   33548             :     "Distance computation is performed in 2d only.\n"
   33549             :     "\n"
   33550             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::segmentize().\n"
   33551             :     "\n"
   33552             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33553             :     "-----------\n"
   33554             :     "\n"
   33555             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to segmentize\n"
   33556             :     "\n"
   33557             :     "dfMaxLength:  the maximum distance between 2 points after\n"
   33558             :     "segmentization \n"
   33559             :     ""},
   33560             :    { (char *)"Geometry_GetEnvelope", _wrap_Geometry_GetEnvelope, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33561             :     "Geometry_GetEnvelope(Geometry self)\n"
   33562             :     "\n"
   33563             :     "void\n"
   33564             :     "OGR_G_GetEnvelope(OGRGeometryH hGeom, OGREnvelope *psEnvelope)\n"
   33565             :     "\n"
   33566             :     "Computes and returns the bounding envelope for this geometry in the\n"
   33567             :     "passed psEnvelope structure.\n"
   33568             :     "\n"
   33569             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method\n"
   33570             :     "OGRGeometry::getEnvelope().\n"
   33571             :     "\n"
   33572             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33573             :     "-----------\n"
   33574             :     "\n"
   33575             :     "hGeom:  handle of the geometry to get envelope from.\n"
   33576             :     "\n"
   33577             :     "psEnvelope:  the structure in which to place the results. \n"
   33578             :     ""},
   33579             :    { (char *)"Geometry_GetEnvelope3D", _wrap_Geometry_GetEnvelope3D, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33580             :     "Geometry_GetEnvelope3D(Geometry self)\n"
   33581             :     "\n"
   33582             :     "void\n"
   33583             :     "OGR_G_GetEnvelope3D(OGRGeometryH hGeom, OGREnvelope3D *psEnvelope)\n"
   33584             :     "\n"
   33585             :     "Computes and returns the bounding envelope (3D) for this geometry in\n"
   33586             :     "the passed psEnvelope structure.\n"
   33587             :     "\n"
   33588             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method\n"
   33589             :     "OGRGeometry::getEnvelope().\n"
   33590             :     "\n"
   33591             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33592             :     "-----------\n"
   33593             :     "\n"
   33594             :     "hGeom:  handle of the geometry to get envelope from.\n"
   33595             :     "\n"
   33596             :     "psEnvelope:  the structure in which to place the results.\n"
   33597             :     "\n"
   33598             :     "OGR 1.9.0 \n"
   33599             :     ""},
   33600             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Centroid", _wrap_Geometry_Centroid, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33601             :     "Geometry_Centroid(Geometry self) -> Geometry\n"
   33602             :     "\n"
   33603             :     "int OGR_G_Centroid(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33604             :     "hGeom, OGRGeometryH hCentroidPoint)\n"
   33605             :     "\n"
   33606             :     "Compute the geometry centroid.\n"
   33607             :     "\n"
   33608             :     "The centroid location is applied to the passed in OGRPoint object. The\n"
   33609             :     "centroid is not necessarily within the geometry.\n"
   33610             :     "\n"
   33611             :     "This method relates to the SFCOM ISurface::get_Centroid() method\n"
   33612             :     "however the current implementation based on GEOS can operate on other\n"
   33613             :     "geometry types such as multipoint, linestring, geometrycollection such\n"
   33614             :     "as multipolygons. OGC SF SQL 1.1 defines the operation for surfaces\n"
   33615             :     "(polygons). SQL/MM-Part 3 defines the operation for surfaces and\n"
   33616             :     "multisurfaces (multipolygons).\n"
   33617             :     "\n"
   33618             :     "This function is the same as the C++ method OGRGeometry::Centroid().\n"
   33619             :     "\n"
   33620             :     "This function is built on the GEOS library, check it for the\n"
   33621             :     "definition of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS\n"
   33622             :     "library, this function will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported\n"
   33623             :     "error.\n"
   33624             :     "\n"
   33625             :     "OGRERR_NONE on success or OGRERR_FAILURE on error. \n"
   33626             :     ""},
   33627             :    { (char *)"Geometry_PointOnSurface", _wrap_Geometry_PointOnSurface, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33628             :     "Geometry_PointOnSurface(Geometry self) -> Geometry\n"
   33629             :     "\n"
   33630             :     "OGRGeometryH\n"
   33631             :     "OGR_G_PointOnSurface(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33632             :     "\n"
   33633             :     "Returns a point guaranteed to lie on the surface.\n"
   33634             :     "\n"
   33635             :     "This method relates to the SFCOM ISurface::get_PointOnSurface() method\n"
   33636             :     "however the current implementation based on GEOS can operate on other\n"
   33637             :     "geometry types than the types that are supported by SQL/MM-Part 3 :\n"
   33638             :     "surfaces (polygons) and multisurfaces (multipolygons).\n"
   33639             :     "\n"
   33640             :     "This method is built on the GEOS library, check it for the definition\n"
   33641             :     "of the geometry operation. If OGR is built without the GEOS library,\n"
   33642             :     "this method will always fail, issuing a CPLE_NotSupported error.\n"
   33643             :     "\n"
   33644             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33645             :     "-----------\n"
   33646             :     "\n"
   33647             :     "hGeom:  the geometry to operate on.\n"
   33648             :     "\n"
   33649             :     "a point guaranteed to lie on the surface or NULL if an error occurred.\n"
   33650             :     "\n"
   33651             :     "OGR 1.10 \n"
   33652             :     ""},
   33653             :    { (char *)"Geometry_WkbSize", _wrap_Geometry_WkbSize, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33654             :     "Geometry_WkbSize(Geometry self) -> int\n"
   33655             :     "\n"
   33656             :     "int OGR_G_WkbSize(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33657             :     "\n"
   33658             :     "Returns size of related binary representation.\n"
   33659             :     "\n"
   33660             :     "This function returns the exact number of bytes required to hold the\n"
   33661             :     "well known binary representation of this geometry object. Its\n"
   33662             :     "computation may be slightly expensive for complex geometries.\n"
   33663             :     "\n"
   33664             :     "This function relates to the SFCOM IWks::WkbSize() method.\n"
   33665             :     "\n"
   33666             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::WkbSize().\n"
   33667             :     "\n"
   33668             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33669             :     "-----------\n"
   33670             :     "\n"
   33671             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to get the binary size from.\n"
   33672             :     "\n"
   33673             :     "size of binary representation in bytes. \n"
   33674             :     ""},
   33675             :    { (char *)"Geometry_GetCoordinateDimension", _wrap_Geometry_GetCoordinateDimension, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33676             :     "Geometry_GetCoordinateDimension(Geometry self) -> int\n"
   33677             :     "\n"
   33678             :     "int\n"
   33679             :     "OGR_G_GetCoordinateDimension(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33680             :     "\n"
   33681             :     "Get the dimension of the coordinates in this geometry.\n"
   33682             :     "\n"
   33683             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method\n"
   33684             :     "OGRGeometry::getCoordinateDimension().\n"
   33685             :     "\n"
   33686             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33687             :     "-----------\n"
   33688             :     "\n"
   33689             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to get the dimension of the coordinates\n"
   33690             :     "from.\n"
   33691             :     "\n"
   33692             :     "Deprecated use OGR_G_CoordinateDimension(), OGR_G_Is3D(), or\n"
   33693             :     "OGR_G_IsMeasured().\n"
   33694             :     "\n"
   33695             :     "this will return 2 or 3. \n"
   33696             :     ""},
   33697             :    { (char *)"Geometry_CoordinateDimension", _wrap_Geometry_CoordinateDimension, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33698             :     "Geometry_CoordinateDimension(Geometry self) -> int\n"
   33699             :     "\n"
   33700             :     "int\n"
   33701             :     "OGR_G_CoordinateDimension(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33702             :     "\n"
   33703             :     "Get the dimension of the coordinates in this geometry.\n"
   33704             :     "\n"
   33705             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method\n"
   33706             :     "OGRGeometry::CoordinateDimension().\n"
   33707             :     "\n"
   33708             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33709             :     "-----------\n"
   33710             :     "\n"
   33711             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to get the dimension of the coordinates\n"
   33712             :     "from.\n"
   33713             :     "\n"
   33714             :     "this will return 2 for XY, 3 for XYZ and XYM, and 4 for XYZM data.\n"
   33715             :     "\n"
   33716             :     "GDAL 2.1 \n"
   33717             :     ""},
   33718             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Is3D", _wrap_Geometry_Is3D, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33719             :     "Geometry_Is3D(Geometry self) -> int\n"
   33720             :     "\n"
   33721             :     "int OGR_G_Is3D(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33722             :     "\n"
   33723             :     "See whether this geometry has Z coordinates.\n"
   33724             :     "\n"
   33725             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::Is3D().\n"
   33726             :     "\n"
   33727             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33728             :     "-----------\n"
   33729             :     "\n"
   33730             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to check whether it has Z coordinates.\n"
   33731             :     "\n"
   33732             :     "TRUE if the geometry has Z coordinates.\n"
   33733             :     "\n"
   33734             :     "GDAL 2.1 \n"
   33735             :     ""},
   33736             :    { (char *)"Geometry_IsMeasured", _wrap_Geometry_IsMeasured, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33737             :     "Geometry_IsMeasured(Geometry self) -> int\n"
   33738             :     "\n"
   33739             :     "int OGR_G_IsMeasured(OGRGeometryH\n"
   33740             :     "hGeom)\n"
   33741             :     "\n"
   33742             :     "See whether this geometry is measured.\n"
   33743             :     "\n"
   33744             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method OGRGeometry::IsMeasured().\n"
   33745             :     "\n"
   33746             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33747             :     "-----------\n"
   33748             :     "\n"
   33749             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to check whether it is measured.\n"
   33750             :     "\n"
   33751             :     "TRUE if the geometry has M coordinates.\n"
   33752             :     "\n"
   33753             :     "GDAL 2.1 \n"
   33754             :     ""},
   33755             :    { (char *)"Geometry_SetCoordinateDimension", _wrap_Geometry_SetCoordinateDimension, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33756             :     "Geometry_SetCoordinateDimension(Geometry self, int dimension)\n"
   33757             :     "\n"
   33758             :     "void\n"
   33759             :     "OGR_G_SetCoordinateDimension(OGRGeometryH hGeom, int nNewDimension)\n"
   33760             :     "\n"
   33761             :     "Set the coordinate dimension.\n"
   33762             :     "\n"
   33763             :     "This method sets the explicit coordinate dimension. Setting the\n"
   33764             :     "coordinate dimension of a geometry to 2 should zero out any existing Z\n"
   33765             :     "values. Setting the dimension of a geometry collection, a compound\n"
   33766             :     "curve, a polygon, etc. will affect the children geometries. This will\n"
   33767             :     "also remove the M dimension if present before this call.\n"
   33768             :     "\n"
   33769             :     "Deprecated use OGR_G_Set3D() or OGR_G_SetMeasured().\n"
   33770             :     "\n"
   33771             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33772             :     "-----------\n"
   33773             :     "\n"
   33774             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to set the dimension of the\n"
   33775             :     "coordinates.\n"
   33776             :     "\n"
   33777             :     "nNewDimension:  New coordinate dimension value, either 2 or 3. \n"
   33778             :     ""},
   33779             :    { (char *)"Geometry_Set3D", _wrap_Geometry_Set3D, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33780             :     "Geometry_Set3D(Geometry self, int b3D)\n"
   33781             :     "\n"
   33782             :     "void OGR_G_Set3D(OGRGeometryH hGeom,\n"
   33783             :     "int bIs3D)\n"
   33784             :     "\n"
   33785             :     "Add or remove the Z coordinate dimension.\n"
   33786             :     "\n"
   33787             :     "This method adds or removes the explicit Z coordinate dimension.\n"
   33788             :     "Removing the Z coordinate dimension of a geometry will remove any\n"
   33789             :     "existing Z values. Adding the Z dimension to a geometry collection, a\n"
   33790             :     "compound curve, a polygon, etc. will affect the children geometries.\n"
   33791             :     "\n"
   33792             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33793             :     "-----------\n"
   33794             :     "\n"
   33795             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to set or unset the Z dimension.\n"
   33796             :     "\n"
   33797             :     "bIs3D:  Should the geometry have a Z dimension, either TRUE or FALSE.\n"
   33798             :     "\n"
   33799             :     "GDAL 2.1 \n"
   33800             :     ""},
   33801             :    { (char *)"Geometry_SetMeasured", _wrap_Geometry_SetMeasured, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33802             :     "Geometry_SetMeasured(Geometry self, int bMeasured)\n"
   33803             :     "\n"
   33804             :     "void\n"
   33805             :     "OGR_G_SetMeasured(OGRGeometryH hGeom, int bIsMeasured)\n"
   33806             :     "\n"
   33807             :     "Add or remove the M coordinate dimension.\n"
   33808             :     "\n"
   33809             :     "This method adds or removes the explicit M coordinate dimension.\n"
   33810             :     "Removing the M coordinate dimension of a geometry will remove any\n"
   33811             :     "existing M values. Adding the M dimension to a geometry collection, a\n"
   33812             :     "compound curve, a polygon, etc. will affect the children geometries.\n"
   33813             :     "\n"
   33814             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33815             :     "-----------\n"
   33816             :     "\n"
   33817             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to set or unset the M dimension.\n"
   33818             :     "\n"
   33819             :     "bIsMeasured:  Should the geometry have a M dimension, either TRUE or\n"
   33820             :     "FALSE.\n"
   33821             :     "\n"
   33822             :     "GDAL 2.1 \n"
   33823             :     ""},
   33824             :    { (char *)"Geometry_GetDimension", _wrap_Geometry_GetDimension, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"\n"
   33825             :     "Geometry_GetDimension(Geometry self) -> int\n"
   33826             :     "\n"
   33827             :     "int\n"
   33828             :     "OGR_G_GetDimension(OGRGeometryH hGeom)\n"
   33829             :     "\n"
   33830             :     "Get the dimension of this geometry.\n"
   33831             :     "\n"
   33832             :     "This function corresponds to the SFCOM IGeometry::GetDimension()\n"
   33833             :     "method. It indicates the dimension of the geometry, but does not\n"
   33834             :     "indicate the dimension of the underlying space (as indicated by\n"
   33835             :     "OGR_G_GetCoordinateDimension() function).\n"
   33836             :     "\n"
   33837             :     "This function is the same as the CPP method\n"
   33838             :     "OGRGeometry::getDimension().\n"
   33839             :     "\n"
   33840             :     "Parameters:\n"
   33841             :     "-----------\n"
   33842             :     "\n"
   33843             :     "hGeom:  handle on the geometry to get the dimension from.\n"
   33844             :     "\n"
   33845             :     "0 for points, 1 for lines and 2 for surfaces. \n"
   33846             :     ""},
   33847             :    { (char *)"Geometry_HasCurveGeometry", _wrap_Geometry_HasCurveGeometry, METH_VARARGS, (char *)"Geometry_HasCurveGeometry(Geometry self, int bLookForCircular=False) -> int"},
   33848             :    { (char *)"Geometry_GetLinearGeometry", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Geometry_GetLinearGeometry, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"Geometry_GetLinearGeometry(Geometry self, double dfMaxAngleStepSizeDegrees=0.0, char ** options=None) -> Geometry"},
   33849             :    { (char *)"Geometry_GetCurveGeometry", (PyCFunction) _wrap_Geometry_GetCurveGeometry, METH_VARARGS | METH_KEYWORDS, (char *)"Geometry_GetCurveGeometry(Geometry self, char ** options=None) -> Geometry"},

   34006             : /* -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   34007             :  * Type initialization:
   34008             :  * This problem is tough by the requirement that no dynamic
   34009             :  * memory is used. Also, since swig_type_info structures store pointers to
   34010             :  * swig_cast_info structures and swig_cast_info structures store pointers back
   34011             :  * to swig_type_info structures, we need some lookup code at initialization.
   34012             :  * The idea is that swig generates all the structures that are needed.
   34013             :  * The runtime then collects these partially filled structures.
   34014             :  * The SWIG_InitializeModule function takes these initial arrays out of
   34015             :  * swig_module, and does all the lookup, filling in the swig_module.types
   34016             :  * array with the correct data and linking the correct swig_cast_info
   34017             :  * structures together.
   34018             :  *
   34019             :  * The generated swig_type_info structures are assigned statically to an initial
   34020             :  * array. We just loop through that array, and handle each type individually.
   34021             :  * First we lookup if this type has been already loaded, and if so, use the
   34022             :  * loaded structure instead of the generated one. Then we have to fill in the
   34023             :  * cast linked list. The cast data is initially stored in something like a
   34024             :  * two-dimensional array. Each row corresponds to a type (there are the same
   34025             :  * number of rows as there are in the swig_type_initial array). Each entry in
   34026             :  * a column is one of the swig_cast_info structures for that type.
   34027             :  * The cast_initial array is actually an array of arrays, because each row has
   34028             :  * a variable number of columns. So to actually build the cast linked list,
   34029             :  * we find the array of casts associated with the type, and loop through it
   34030             :  * adding the casts to the list. The one last trick we need to do is making
   34031             :  * sure the type pointer in the swig_cast_info struct is correct.
   34032             :  *
   34033             :  * First off, we lookup the cast->type name to see if it is already loaded.
   34034             :  * There are three cases to handle:
   34035             :  *  1) If the cast->type has already been loaded AND the type we are adding
   34036             :  *     casting info to has not been loaded (it is in this module), THEN we
   34037             :  *     replace the cast->type pointer with the type pointer that has already
   34038             :  *     been loaded.
   34039             :  *  2) If BOTH types (the one we are adding casting info to, and the
   34040             :  *     cast->type) are loaded, THEN the cast info has already been loaded by
   34041             :  *     the previous module so we just ignore it.
   34042             :  *  3) Finally, if cast->type has not already been loaded, then we add that
   34043             :  *     swig_cast_info to the linked list (because the cast->type) pointer will
   34044             :  *     be correct.
   34045             :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   34046             : 
   34047             : #ifdef __cplusplus
   34048             : extern "C" {
   34049             : #if 0
   34050             : } /* c-mode */
   34051             : #endif
   34052             : #endif
   34053             : 
   34054             : #if 0
   34055             : #define SWIGRUNTIME_DEBUG
   34056             : #endif

   34062             :   swig_module_info *module_head, *iter;

   34070         179 :     swig_module.next = &swig_module;
   34071         179 :     init = 1;

   34073             :     init = 0;
